items.hr: Deve compilare questo modulo e conservare la ricevuta con il codice di tracciamento.
items.en: You have to fill in this form and keep the receipt with the tracking number.
items.note: il modulo, la ricevuta, il codice di tracciamento

items.hr: Vengo a pagare un bollettino e a ritirare una raccomandata.
items.en: I am here to pay a bill and to collect a registered letter.
items.note: il bollettino is the paper slip Italians use to pay bills at the counter

activities.title: Spedire e ritirare
activities.intro: Practise the counter vocabulary and revise the relative pronoun.

questions.prompt: Lei spedisce un documento importante e vuole una prova della spedizione. Che cosa chiede allo sportello?
questions.explanation: La raccomandata is registered post: it is tracked and signed for, so it gives you proof that the item arrived.

questions.prompt: Completa: Il pacco ___ ho spedito lunedì è già arrivato.
questions.explanation: The invariable relative che links il pacco to the verb ho spedito, and Italian cannot leave it out even though English can.

questions.prompt: Completa: Buongiorno, vorrei ___ questo pacco in Spagna.
questions.explanation: After vorrei you need the infinitive of the verb that means to send something by post.

questions.prompt: Completa: Alla fine l'impiegato mi dà la ___ con il codice di tracciamento.
questions.explanation: The slip of paper proving you paid and carrying the tracking number is feminine, so it takes la.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I would like to send this parcel by registered post.'
questions.explanation: Per raccomandata is the fixed expression for the postal service used, and it takes no article.

activities.title: Un pacco per Bologna
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are at the post office counter with a parcel, and everything is in the Lei form.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, mi dica.
lines.en: Good morning, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno, vorrei spedire questo pacco a Bologna.
lines.en: Good morning, I would like to send this parcel to Bologna.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lo mettiamo sulla bilancia. Pesa tre chili. Preferisce una raccomandata?
lines.en: Let's put it on the scales. It weighs three kilos. Do you prefer registered post?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, grazie. Vorrei anche il codice di tracciamento.
lines.en: Yes, thank you. I would also like the tracking number.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo, è stampato sulla ricevuta. Intanto compili questo modulo con l'indirizzo.
lines.en: Of course, it is printed on the receipt. In the meantime fill in this form with the address.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ecco fatto. Posso pagare anche un bollettino qui?
lines.en: There you go. Can I also pay a bill here?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, ma allo sportello accanto. Le servono dei francobolli?
lines.en: Yes, but at the next counter. Do you need any stamps?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, grazie. Passo più tardi a ritirare una raccomandata.
lines.en: No, thank you. I will come by later to collect a registered letter.

days.title: Cercare casa, l'annuncio
days.objective: Read a typical Italian rental advert and say what kind of flat you are looking for.
days.paretoFocus: Rental adverts use a small, fixed set of words and abbreviations, so learning twenty of them unlocks almost every listing you will ever read.
drills: Describe your ideal flat in three sentences: size, floor, and what it must have.
Write a short advert for the place you live in now.
Say aloud your monthly budget and whether bills are included.

items: How do you say that the rent does not include the bills?
What does a bilocale contain?
Which article goes before studio, and why?

activities.title: Leggere un annuncio di casa
activities.intro: Italian adverts count rooms, not bedrooms: a bilocale has two rooms in total. The rent is il canone, and the key phrase is spese incluse or spese escluse, meaning bills included or not. Use the conditional to sound polite when you enquire.

items.hr: Cerco un bilocale arredato in centro.
items.en: I am looking for a furnished two-room flat in the centre.
items.note: un bilocale = a living room plus one bedroom

items.hr: L'annuncio dice: quarto piano con ascensore.
items.en: The advert says: fourth floor with a lift.

items.hr: Il canone è di seicento euro al mese, spese escluse.
items.en: The rent is six hundred euros a month, bills not included.
items.note: il canone = the rent; spese escluse = bills paid separately

items.hr: Mi interesserebbe vedere l'appartamento questa settimana.
items.en: I would be interested in seeing the flat this week.
items.note: the conditional interesserebbe softens the request

items.hr: Lo studio è piccolo ma luminoso e silenzioso.
items.en: The studio flat is small but bright and quiet.
items.note: lo before s plus consonant: lo studio

activities.title: Capire l'annuncio
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of rental adverts and the article before s plus consonant.

questions.prompt: In an advert, spese escluse tells you that:
options: the bills are paid on top of the rent
questions.answer: the bills are paid on top of the rent
questions.explanation: Escluse means excluded, so heating, water and building charges are paid separately from il canone.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct article: ___ studio si trova al secondo piano.
questions.explanation: Masculine nouns beginning with s plus a consonant take lo, so lo studio, lo sconto, lo specchio.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conditional of volere (io form): ___ vedere l'appartamento domani mattina.
questions.explanation: Vorrei is the conditional of volere and is the normal polite way to ask for something.

questions.prompt: Complete the advert sentence with the technical word for the monthly rent: il ___ è di settecento euro.
questions.explanation: Il canone di locazione is the formal word for rent in adverts and contracts; affitto is the everyday word.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am looking for a furnished flat in the centre.'
questions.explanation: The adjective arredato follows the noun and agrees with it, masculine singular.

activities.title: Telefonata per un annuncio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You phone about a flat you saw advertised and ask about the rent and the bills, using the conditional to stay polite.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, mi ha chiamato per l'appartamento?
lines.en: Good morning, are you calling about the flat?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, buongiorno. Vorrei qualche informazione sull'annuncio.
lines.en: Yes, good morning. I would like some information about the advert.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo. È un bilocale arredato al terzo piano.
lines.en: Of course. It is a furnished two-room flat on the third floor.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il canone di seicento euro è con le spese incluse?
lines.en: Is the rent of six hundred euros with the bills included?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: No, le spese condominiali sono a parte, circa quaranta euro al mese.
lines.en: No, the building charges are separate, about forty euros a month.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco. Mi interesserebbe vederlo giovedì pomeriggio, se possibile.
lines.en: I see. I would be interested in seeing it on Thursday afternoon, if possible.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene, ci vediamo giovedì alle cinque davanti al portone.
lines.en: All right, we will meet on Thursday at five in front of the main door.

days.title: Il contratto d'affitto e le spese
days.objective: Understand the main parts of an Italian rental contract and ask what you have to pay before moving in.
days.paretoFocus: The deposit, the building charges and the registration of the contract are where newcomers lose money, so understanding the words once protects you for years.
drills: List in Italian everything you pay in the first month of a tenancy.
Explain in one sentence what la caparra is and when you get it back.
Ask three questions you would put to a landlord before signing.

items: What is la caparra and how much is it usually?
What do le spese condominiali cover?
Are the utilities normally inside the rent or separate?

activities.title: Firmare il contratto
activities.intro: A tenancy contract must be registered, and the landlord may choose a flat-rate tax option called la cedolare secca, which you only need to recognise. You almost always pay a deposit, la caparra, usually two or three months of rent. Building charges are separate, and gas and electricity are usually in your own name.

items.hr: Il contratto d'affitto dura quattro anni e si può rinnovare.
items.en: The rental contract lasts four years and can be renewed.

items.hr: Alla firma ho pagato una caparra di due mensilità.
items.en: On signing I paid a deposit of two months of rent.
items.note: la caparra = the security deposit, returned at the end if there is no damage

items.hr: Le spese condominiali coprono la pulizia delle scale e l'ascensore.
items.en: The building charges cover the cleaning of the stairs and the lift.

items.hr: Il proprietario ha scelto la cedolare secca per le tasse.
items.en: The landlord chose the flat-rate tax option for the taxes.
items.note: la cedolare secca is the landlord's tax choice, not yours

items.hr: Le utenze di luce e gas sono a mio carico.
items.en: The electricity and gas bills are my responsibility.
items.note: a mio carico = paid by me

activities.title: Le parole del contratto
activities.intro: Practise the money words of a tenancy and the agreement of past participles.

questions.prompt: You get your caparra back at the end of the tenancy when:
options: you leave the flat in good condition
you have lived there four years
you paid the rent by bank transfer
questions.answer: you leave the flat in good condition
questions.explanation: La caparra is security against damage and unpaid rent, so it is returned if the flat is handed back in order.

questions.prompt: Which sentence has the correct participle agreement?
options: Le spese le ho pagate ieri.
Le spese le ho pagato ieri.
Le spese le ho pagati ieri.
Le spese le ho pagata ieri.
questions.answer: Le spese le ho pagate ieri.
questions.explanation: With a direct object pronoun before avere the participle agrees with it, and le spese is feminine plural, so pagate.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for the shared building charges: le ___ condominiali sono di quaranta euro al mese.
questions.explanation: Le spese condominiali are the shared costs of the building, always plural.

questions.prompt: Complete: le utenze di luce e gas sono a mio ___ , quindi le pago io.
questions.explanation: A mio carico is the fixed expression meaning at my expense.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'On signing I paid a deposit of two months of rent.'
questions.explanation: Passato prossimo with avere: the participle pagato stays unchanged because no object pronoun comes before it.

activities.title: Prima di firmare
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are about to sign a tenancy and you check what you must pay and what is included.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se l'appartamento le piace, possiamo firmare la settimana prossima.
lines.en: If you like the flat, we can sign next week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Volentieri. Quanto devo pagare al momento della firma?
lines.en: Gladly. How much do I have to pay at the moment of signing?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il primo mese più una caparra di due mensilità.
lines.en: The first month plus a deposit of two months of rent.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E le spese condominiali sono comprese nel canone?
lines.en: And are the building charges included in the rent?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: No, sono a parte. Anche luce e gas sono a suo carico.
lines.en: No, they are separate. Electricity and gas are your responsibility too.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accordo. Il contratto viene registrato, vero?
lines.en: All right. The contract does get registered, doesn't it?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo, lo registro io e le mando una copia.
lines.en: Of course, I register it myself and I will send you a copy.

days.title: Il trasloco e i servizi
days.objective: Organise a move and get the electricity, gas and internet connected in your name.
days.paretoFocus: Moving day language is nearly all future tense plus a handful of utility words, and getting the meter reading right saves you a bill you do not owe.
drills: Plan your move out loud using the future: five things you will do.
Say in Italian what you would do first if the flat had no electricity on arrival.
Write the questions you would ask to transfer a contract into your name.

items: Which word means transferring a utility contract to a new holder?
How do you say the meter?
Give the future of fare in the io and noi forms.

activities.title: Il giorno del trasloco
activities.intro: For a move, Italians use the future tense freely to plan. The two key utility words are l'allacciamento, connecting a supply for the first time, and la voltura, moving an existing contract into your name. Always note il contatore, the meter reading, on the day.

items.hr: Sabato faremo il trasloco con un furgone a noleggio.
items.en: On Saturday we will move with a rented van.
items.note: faremo = future of fare, noi form

items.hr: Devo chiedere la voltura del contratto della luce.
items.en: I have to ask for the electricity contract to be put in my name.
items.note: la voltura = change of contract holder

items.hr: L'allacciamento del gas richiede qualche giorno.
items.en: Connecting the gas takes a few days.

items.hr: Ho letto il contatore e ho fotografato i numeri.
items.en: I read the meter and I photographed the numbers.
items.note: il contatore = the meter

items.hr: L'idraulico verrà lunedì per controllare gli scarichi.
items.en: The plumber will come on Monday to check the drains.
items.note: verrà = future of venire, lui form

activities.title: Attivare le utenze
activities.intro: Practise the future tense and the words for connecting services.

questions.prompt: The supply already exists and you simply want the contract in your name. You ask for:
questions.explanation: Voltura transfers an existing contract; allacciamento creates a new physical connection.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct future of venire for lui?
questions.explanation: Venire is irregular in the future: verrò, verrai, verrà, with a double r.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future of fare (noi form): Sabato ___ il trasloco.
questions.explanation: Fare keeps the a in its future stem: farò, farai, farà, faremo.

questions.prompt: Complete with the device that records how much gas you use: prima di uscire ho letto il ___ .
questions.explanation: Il contatore is the meter; noting the reading protects you from paying the previous tenant's usage.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The plumber will come on Monday morning.'
questions.explanation: Days of the week take no preposition when they refer to one specific occasion.

activities.title: La luce nella casa nuova
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You call to put the electricity contract into your name and give the meter reading.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Servizio clienti, buongiorno. Come posso aiutarla?
lines.en: Customer service, good morning. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno. Mi sono trasferito e vorrei chiedere la voltura della luce.
lines.en: Good morning. I have moved and I would like to ask for the electricity contract to be transferred.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo. Ha con sé il numero del contatore?
lines.en: Of course. Do you have the meter number with you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, l'ho fotografato stamattina. Le leggo anche la lettura.
lines.en: Yes, I photographed it this morning. I will read you the reading as well.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfetto. La pratica sarà pronta entro cinque giorni lavorativi.
lines.en: Perfect. The application will be ready within five working days.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E per il gas dovrò fare una richiesta separata?
lines.en: And for the gas will I have to make a separate request?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, per il gas serve una domanda a parte.
lines.en: Yes, for the gas a separate application is needed.

days.title: I vicini e il condominio
days.objective: Introduce yourself to neighbours and understand how an Italian block of flats is run.
days.paretoFocus: Almost every Italian flat sits inside a condominio with an administrator and meetings, and a good first impression with the neighbours makes daily life far easier.
drills: Introduce yourself to a neighbour in three sentences using Lei.
Write a short polite note asking neighbours to keep the entrance door closed.
Say what you would raise at a building meeting.

items: Who is l'amministratore and what do they do?
What happens at l'assemblea condominiale?
How do you politely ask a neighbour to lower the volume?

activities.title: Conoscere i vicini
activities.intro: A condominio is the community of owners in a building; it has an administrator and holds meetings. With neighbours you do not know, start with Lei and let them offer the tu. Polite requests use the formal imperative or the conditional.

items.hr: Piacere, sono il nuovo inquilino del terzo piano.
items.en: Nice to meet you, I am the new tenant on the third floor.
items.note: l'inquilino = the tenant, il proprietario = the owner

items.hr: L'amministratore convoca l'assemblea due volte all'anno.
items.en: The administrator calls the meeting twice a year.

items.hr: Chiuda il portone, per favore, altrimenti resta aperto tutta la notte.
items.en: Please close the main door, otherwise it stays open all night.
items.note: chiuda = formal imperative of chiudere

items.hr: Potrebbe abbassare un po' la musica dopo le undici?
items.en: Could you turn the music down a little after eleven?
items.note: potrebbe softens the request more than the imperative

items.hr: Ci vediamo spesso sul pianerottolo o in cortile.
items.en: We often see each other on the landing or in the courtyard.

activities.title: Vita di condominio
activities.intro: Practise the formal imperative and the vocabulary of the building.

questions.prompt: L'amministratore di condominio is the person who:
options: manages the shared costs and calls the meetings
owns all the flats in the building
cleans the stairs and the courtyard
collects the rent from every tenant
questions.answer: manages the shared costs and calls the meetings
questions.explanation: The administrator handles shared accounts, maintenance and the assemblea; the owner of each flat is a separate person.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct formal imperative of chiudere:
questions.explanation: Verbs in ere form the formal imperative in a: chiuda, prenda, scriva.

questions.prompt: Complete with the formal imperative of scusare: ___ il disturbo, sono il vicino di sopra.
questions.explanation: Verbs in are take i in the formal imperative: scusi, parli, aspetti.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for the small shared landing outside the flats: ci siamo incontrati sul ___ .
questions.explanation: Il pianerottolo is the landing shared by the flats on one floor.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Could you turn the music down a little?'
questions.explanation: Potrebbe plus the infinitive is the standard polite request to someone you address as Lei.

activities.title: Sul pianerottolo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You meet a neighbour for the first time and ask how the building works.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buonasera. Lei è la persona che si è trasferita al terzo?
lines.en: Good evening. Are you the person who moved in on the third floor?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, piacere. Sono il nuovo inquilino, mi sono trasferito sabato.
lines.en: Yes, nice to meet you. I am the new tenant, I moved in on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Benvenuto. Se ha bisogno di qualcosa, io abito qui accanto.
lines.en: Welcome. If you need anything, I live right next door.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grazie. Mi scusi, quando si tiene l'assemblea di condominio?
lines.en: Thank you. Excuse me, when is the building meeting held?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: In genere a maggio. L'amministratore manda l'avviso prima.
lines.en: Usually in May. The administrator sends the notice beforehand.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfetto. Una cosa: il portone resta spesso aperto la sera.
lines.en: Perfect. One thing: the main door often stays open in the evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lo so, ne parliamo sempre. Lo dirò anche agli altri.
lines.en: I know, we always talk about it. I will mention it to the others too.

days.title: La raccolta differenziata
days.objective: Sort household waste into the right categories and talk about the collection days.
days.paretoFocus: Sorting rules are enforced in most Italian towns, and the five category words appear on every bin, calendar and notice in the building.
drills: Name the bin for ten things you threw away this week.
Say which day each category is collected where you live.
Explain to a guest in Italian where the batteries go.

items: What goes in l'umido?
What is l'indifferenziato?
Use ci in a sentence about the bins downstairs.

activities.title: Dove butto questo?
activities.intro: Italian towns separate waste into carta, plastica, vetro, umido and indifferenziato, each with its own bin and collection day. Use the pronoun ci to replace a place already mentioned, and ne to replace a quantity. The verb is buttare or gettare.

items.hr: L'umido si butta ogni martedì e ogni venerdì.
items.en: The food waste is put out every Tuesday and every Friday.
items.note: l'umido = wet organic waste

items.hr: La carta e il cartone vanno nel bidone bianco.
items.en: Paper and cardboard go in the white bin.

items.hr: Il vetro lo porto giù una volta alla settimana.
items.en: The glass I take down once a week.
items.note: lo replaces il vetro and stands before the verb

items.hr: Nell'indifferenziato ci va solo quello che non si può riciclare.
items.en: In the general waste goes only what cannot be recycled.
items.note: ci refers back to nell'indifferenziato

items.hr: Le pile sono pericolose, quindi non ne metto mai nel sacco.
items.en: Batteries are dangerous, so I never put any of them in the bag.
items.note: ne = of them

activities.title: Ogni cosa al suo posto
activities.intro: Practise the waste categories and the pronouns ci and ne.

questions.prompt: Leftover vegetable peel goes in:
questions.explanation: L'umido collects organic kitchen waste, which is composted.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses ne correctly?
options: Di bottiglie ne ho buttate tre.
Di bottiglie ci ho buttate tre.
Di bottiglie le ho ne buttate tre.
Di bottiglie ne ho buttato tre.
questions.answer: Di bottiglie ne ho buttate tre.
questions.explanation: Ne replaces a quantity and the participle agrees with what ne refers to, so buttate for bottiglie.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pronoun that replaces a place already mentioned: Il cortile è comodo, ___ scendo ogni sera con il sacco.
questions.explanation: Ci stands for a place already named, here in cortile.

questions.prompt: Complete with the category for waste that cannot be recycled: il resto va nell' ___ .
questions.explanation: L'indifferenziato is what is left once the recyclable categories have been removed.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The glass I take down once a week.'
questions.explanation: When the object is moved to the front for emphasis, Italian repeats it as a pronoun before the verb.

activities.title: Il calendario dei rifiuti
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour explains the collection days and you check where two things go.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha visto il calendario della raccolta vicino alle cassette?
lines.en: Have you seen the collection calendar next to the letterboxes?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non ancora. Quando si mette fuori l'umido?
lines.en: Not yet. When do you put the food waste out?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Martedì e venerdì, dopo le otto di sera.
lines.en: Tuesday and Friday, after eight in the evening.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E il vetro? Ne ho già parecchio da buttare.
lines.en: And the glass? I already have quite a lot of it to throw away.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il vetro va nella campana in strada, sempre aperta.
lines.en: The glass goes in the container in the street, always open.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfetto, ci passo domani mattina prima di uscire.
lines.en: Perfect, I will stop by there tomorrow morning before going out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bravo. Le pile invece si portano in un contenitore apposta.
lines.en: Well done. Batteries, on the other hand, are taken to a special container.

days.title: Segnalare un guasto
days.objective: Report something broken in your flat and arrange for a technician to come.
days.paretoFocus: Two expressions, non funziona and si è rotto, plus a time phrase with da, cover nearly every fault you will ever have to report.
drills: Report three faults in your home in Italian, each in one sentence.
Say how long a problem has been going on using da.
Ask a landlord in writing to send a technician this week.

items: Difference between non funziona and si è rotto?
How do you say the boiler has been broken for two days?
Give a polite conditional way to ask someone to send a technician.

activities.title: Non funziona niente
activities.intro: Use non funziona for something that will not work and si è rotto for something that broke. For a problem still going on, use da plus a length of time with the present tense. C'è una perdita means there is a leak.

items.hr: La caldaia non funziona da due giorni.
items.en: The boiler has not been working for two days.
items.note: present tense plus da for something still true now

items.hr: Si è rotta la serratura della porta d'ingresso.
items.en: The lock of the front door has broken.
items.note: rotta agrees with la serratura

items.hr: C'è una perdita d'acqua sotto il lavandino.
items.en: There is a water leak under the sink.

items.hr: Potrebbe mandare un tecnico entro giovedì?
items.en: Could you send a technician by Thursday?

items.hr: L'interruttore fa scattare il salvavita ogni sera.
items.en: The switch trips the circuit breaker every evening.
items.note: il salvavita = the safety cut-out switch

activities.title: Un guasto in casa
activities.intro: Practise reporting faults and saying how long they have lasted.

questions.prompt: The heating stopped working on Monday and it is now Wednesday. You say:
options: Il riscaldamento non funziona da lunedì.
Il riscaldamento non ha funzionato lunedì.
Il riscaldamento non funzionerà da lunedì.
questions.answer: Il riscaldamento non funziona da lunedì.
questions.explanation: A situation that started in the past and is still true takes the present tense with da.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct agreement: Si è ___ la lavatrice.
questions.explanation: With essere the participle agrees with the subject, and la lavatrice is feminine singular.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for water escaping from a pipe: c'è una ___ sotto il lavandino.
questions.explanation: Una perdita is a leak; the related verb is perdere, as in il rubinetto perde.

questions.prompt: Complete with the preposition used for a period still going on: l'ascensore non funziona ___ tre giorni.
questions.explanation: Da plus the present tense expresses a period that began earlier and continues now.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Could you send a technician tomorrow morning?'
questions.explanation: The conditional potrebbe keeps the request polite while still naming a clear deadline.

activities.title: Chiamo il proprietario
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You call the owner of your flat to report a broken boiler and press for an earlier date.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pronto, mi dica. Qualche problema in casa?
lines.en: Hello, tell me. Some problem in the flat?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Purtroppo sì. La caldaia non funziona da due giorni.
lines.en: Unfortunately yes. The boiler has not been working for two days.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi dispiace. Esce acqua o si spegne e basta?
lines.en: I am sorry. Does water come out or does it just switch off?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si spegne subito e c'è anche una piccola perdita sotto.
lines.en: It switches off immediately and there is also a small leak underneath.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora chiamo il tecnico. Proverà a passare la settimana prossima.
lines.en: Then I will call the technician. He will try to come next week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Potrebbe venire prima? Senza acqua calda è complicato.
lines.en: Could he come sooner? Without hot water it is difficult.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha ragione. Gli chiedo di venire giovedì mattina.
lines.en: You are right. I will ask him to come on Thursday morning.

days.title: All'anagrafe, la residenza
days.objective: Register your address at the town council and explain what you need the residence for.
days.paretoFocus: Residenza is the key that opens the health service, the identity card and most public paperwork, so this single errand unlocks the rest of official life.
drills: Say in Italian why you are going to the registry office.
List the documents you would bring to register an address.
Practise spelling your surname aloud letter by letter.

items: What is la residenza and what does it let you do?
Which office at the comune handles it?
Say the formal sentence for I would like to register my address.

activities.title: Iscriversi all'anagrafe
activities.intro: Your residenza is your officially registered address, held by the anagrafe office of your comune. Once you are registered you can obtain certificates and an identity card and use local services, and an officer may call round to check that you really live there. Always use Lei at the counter.

items.hr: Vorrei iscrivermi all'anagrafe del comune.
items.en: I would like to register with the registry office of the town council.
items.note: iscriversi a = to register with

items.hr: Ho portato il documento d'identità e il contratto d'affitto.
items.en: I brought my identity document and the rental contract.

items.hr: Con la residenza posso richiedere la carta d'identità.
items.en: With my residence registered I can apply for the identity card.

items.hr: Un agente passerà a casa per verificare la residenza.
items.en: An officer will come to the flat to check the residence.
items.note: the check on the address is a normal step

items.hr: Mi può rilasciare un certificato di residenza, per favore?
items.en: Can you issue me a certificate of residence, please?
items.note: rilasciare = to issue, used for official documents

activities.title: Allo sportello del comune
activities.intro: Practise the language of the registry office and reflexive verbs with Lei.

questions.prompt: La residenza is:
options: the address officially registered with your town council
the building where you rent a flat
a permit that allows you to work
the tax you pay on your home
questions.answer: the address officially registered with your town council
questions.explanation: Residenza is the registered address held by the comune, and it is what most public offices check.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct with the formal Lei?
options: Mi potete rilasciare un certificato, Lei?
Mi può rilasciare un certificato, tu?
questions.explanation: Lei takes the third person singular, so può and not puoi.

questions.prompt: Complete with the office that keeps the register of residents: sono andato all' ___ del comune.
questions.explanation: L'anagrafe is the office that records who lives in the town.

questions.prompt: Complete with the reflexive infinitive plus its pronoun (io): vorrei ___ all'anagrafe.
questions.explanation: With a modal plus infinitive the reflexive pronoun attaches to the infinitive, which drops its final e.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'With residence I can apply for the identity card.'
questions.explanation: Richiedere means to apply for, while chiedere on its own would only mean to ask.

activities.title: Cambio di residenza
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. At the town council counter you register your new address, using Lei throughout.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, si accomodi. Di cosa ha bisogno?
lines.en: Good morning, take a seat. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno. Vorrei iscrivermi all'anagrafe, mi sono trasferito da poco.
lines.en: Good morning. I would like to register with the registry office, I moved recently.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha il documento d'identità e un titolo per l'alloggio?
lines.en: Do you have an identity document and proof of the accommodation?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, ecco il documento e una copia del contratto d'affitto.
lines.en: Yes, here is the document and a copy of the rental contract.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bene. Un agente passerà a verificare l'indirizzo entro poche settimane.
lines.en: Good. An officer will come to check the address within a few weeks.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accordo. Poi potrò richiedere la carta d'identità qui?
lines.en: All right. Afterwards will I be able to apply for the identity card here?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, ma con un appuntamento a parte. Le lascio il modulo.
lines.en: Yes, but with a separate appointment. I will leave you the form.

days.title: Prendere e spostare un appuntamento
days.objective: Book an appointment and then move or cancel it politely by phone.
days.paretoFocus: Life in Italy runs on appointments, and the verbs spostare, anticipare and disdire let you change any of them without giving offence.
drills: Book an appointment for next week out loud, offering two possible times.
Move an existing appointment two days later, with a reason.
Cancel an appointment politely and apologise for the short notice.

items: Which verb means to move an appointment to an earlier time?
How do you cancel without sounding rude?
Say I would like to move Thursday's appointment.

activities.title: Spostare l'appuntamento
activities.intro: Prendere un appuntamento is to book one, spostare is to move it, anticipare to bring it forward and posticipare to push it back. Disdire means to cancel. The conditional plus a short reason is the polite formula.

items.hr: Vorrei prendere un appuntamento per la settimana prossima.
items.en: I would like to book an appointment for next week.

items.hr: Potrei spostare l'appuntamento di giovedì a venerdì?
items.en: Could I move Thursday's appointment to Friday?

items.hr: Se possibile, preferirei anticiparlo alla mattina.
items.en: If possible, I would prefer to bring it forward to the morning.
items.note: anticiparlo = anticipare plus lo attached to the infinitive

items.hr: Purtroppo devo disdire, mi è uscito un impegno di lavoro.
items.en: Unfortunately I have to cancel, a work commitment has come up.

items.hr: Mi scusi per il preavviso così breve.
items.en: I am sorry for such short notice.
items.note: il preavviso = advance notice

activities.title: Cambiare data e ora
activities.intro: Practise the verbs for booking, moving and cancelling.

questions.prompt: Your appointment is at five and you want it at nine in the morning instead. You ask to:
questions.explanation: Anticipare moves something to an earlier time, while posticipare moves it later.

questions.prompt: Which is the politest way to cancel?
options: Mi dispiace, purtroppo devo disdire l'appuntamento.
questions.answer: Mi dispiace, purtroppo devo disdire l'appuntamento.
questions.explanation: An apology plus purtroppo plus dovere presents the cancellation as something forced on you rather than a choice.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning to change something to another date: potrei ___ l'appuntamento a lunedì?
questions.explanation: Spostare is the neutral verb for changing the date or the time of a booking.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for warning given in advance: mi scusi per il ___ così breve.
questions.explanation: Il preavviso is the notice given before a change; con poco preavviso means at short notice.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I would like to book an appointment for next week.'
questions.explanation: Italian says prendere un appuntamento, literally to take an appointment.

activities.title: Una telefonata alla segreteria
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You call to move an appointment and settle on a new time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Segreteria, buongiorno. Come posso aiutarla?
lines.en: Reception, good morning. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno. Ho un appuntamento giovedì alle quindici.
lines.en: Good morning. I have an appointment on Thursday at three.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Un attimo che controllo. Sì, lo vedo qui.
lines.en: One moment while I check. Yes, I can see it here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Potrei spostarlo? Quel giorno devo lavorare fino a tardi.
lines.en: Could I move it? That day I have to work until late.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo. Le va bene venerdì alle nove e trenta?
lines.en: Of course. Does Friday at nine thirty suit you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Benissimo, così lo anticipo anche di qualche ora.
lines.en: Very good, that way I also bring it forward by a few hours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fatto. Le mando la conferma con un messaggio.
lines.en: Done. I will send you the confirmation by message.

days.title: Compilare un modulo
days.objective: Fill in an Italian official form correctly, field by field.
days.paretoFocus: The same fifteen field labels appear on every form in Italy, so reading them once means you never have to guess at a counter again.
drills: Write out your own details under the eight standard field labels.
Practise saying your date and place of birth in Italian.
Dictate your address aloud as if a clerk were writing it down.

items: What is the difference between nome and cognome?
What does in stampatello mean?
How do you ask where to sign?

activities.title: Nome, cognome e firma
activities.intro: Italian forms put cognome, the surname, before nome, the first name, so check the order carefully. Luogo e data di nascita means place and date of birth, and il codice fiscale is your tax code. Instructions often say to write in stampatello, in block capitals.

items.hr: Scriva cognome e nome in stampatello.
items.en: Write your surname and first name in block capitals.
items.note: cognome comes first on Italian forms

items.hr: Nel modulo c'è anche il luogo e la data di nascita.
items.en: On the form there is also the place and date of birth.

items.hr: Il codice fiscale si scrive senza spazi.
items.en: The tax code is written without spaces.

items.hr: Barri la casella se è già residente qui.
items.en: Tick the box if you are already resident here.
items.note: barrare la casella = to tick the box

items.hr: Metta la firma in fondo alla pagina, per favore.
items.en: Put your signature at the bottom of the page, please.

activities.title: Campo per campo
activities.intro: Practise reading form labels and following written instructions.

questions.prompt: On an Italian form, the field cognome asks for:
questions.explanation: Cognome is the family name and nome is the given name, and Italian forms usually list cognome first.

questions.prompt: The instruction scrivere in stampatello means:
questions.explanation: Stampatello is printed lettering, as opposed to corsivo, which is joined handwriting.

questions.prompt: Complete with the formal imperative of the verb meaning to tick: ___ la casella se ha già un documento italiano.
questions.explanation: Barrare is an are verb, so the formal imperative ends in i: barri.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for what you write by hand at the bottom of the page: manca la sua ___ .
questions.explanation: La firma is the signature and firmare is the verb.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The tax code is written without spaces.'
questions.explanation: Si plus the third person gives the impersonal form used in written instructions.

activities.title: Mi aiuta con il modulo?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A clerk hands you a form and you check two fields before signing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ecco il modulo. Lo compili in stampatello, per favore.
lines.en: Here is the form. Fill it in in block capitals, please.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Va bene. Qui devo scrivere prima il cognome?
lines.en: All right. Here do I have to write the surname first?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Esatto, cognome e poi nome. Sotto ci va la data di nascita.
lines.en: Exactly, surname and then first name. Underneath goes the date of birth.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E questa casella? Non ho capito che cosa chiede.
lines.en: And this box? I did not understand what it is asking.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La barri solo se è già residente in questo comune.
lines.en: Tick it only if you are already resident in this town.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non lo sono ancora, quindi la lascio vuota. Dove firmo?
lines.en: I am not yet, so I will leave it empty. Where do I sign?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La firma va in fondo, a destra, sopra la riga.
lines.en: The signature goes at the bottom, on the right, above the line.

days.title: Chiedere di ripetere e di spiegare
days.objective: Interrupt politely and get someone to repeat, slow down, spell a word or explain what it means.
days.paretoFocus: This is the survival skill that keeps every other conversation alive: if you can stop someone and make them repeat, you never have to nod at something you did not understand.
drills: Say the five rescue phrases out loud until they come without thinking.
Next time you listen to Italian, pause and ask aloud what one word means.
Practise asking someone to spell a surname and writing it down.

items: How do you ask someone to speak more slowly?
How do you ask what a word means?
How do you ask someone to spell it?

activities.title: Scusi, non ho capito
activities.intro: You are allowed to interrupt. Italians expect it and will slow down happily if you ask. Learn these five sentences by heart and use them the moment you lose the thread, not three sentences later. Use Lei in offices and shops.

items.hr: Scusi, non ho capito.
items.en: Sorry, I did not understand.
items.note: the single most useful sentence in the language

items.hr: Può ripetere, per favore?
items.en: Can you repeat that, please?

items.hr: Può parlare più lentamente?
items.en: Can you speak more slowly?
items.note: più lentamente, or più piano in everyday speech

items.hr: Che cosa significa questa parola?
items.en: What does this word mean?
items.note: significare = to mean; vuol dire is the everyday alternative

items.hr: Come si scrive, per favore?
items.en: How do you spell it, please?
items.note: literally how does one write it

activities.title: Farsi ripetere
activities.intro: Practise the rescue phrases and the polite forms that carry them.

questions.prompt: The clerk is talking far too fast. You say:
questions.explanation: Lentamente is about speed; forte is about volume, which is a different problem.

questions.prompt: You want the meaning of a word you have just heard. You ask:
questions.explanation: Significare asks for the meaning; come si dice asks how to say something you already have in mind.

questions.prompt: Complete the request to hear it again: ___ ripetere, per favore?
questions.explanation: Può is the Lei form of potere; potrebbe is the softer conditional and works just as well.

questions.prompt: Complete the question about the letters of a name: come si ___ , per favore?
questions.explanation: Come si scrive uses the impersonal si and is the standard way to ask for a spelling.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Sorry, I did not understand the last word.'
questions.explanation: Non goes before the auxiliary avere, never between avere and the participle.

activities.title: Mi scusi, come ha detto?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone gives you information too quickly and you stop them four times until you have it all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora, deve presentare la richiesta entro il quindici del mese prossimo.
lines.en: So, you have to submit the application by the fifteenth of next month.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi scusi, non ho capito. Può ripetere più lentamente?
lines.en: Excuse me, I did not understand. Can you repeat that more slowly?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo. La richiesta va presentata entro il quindici.
lines.en: Of course. The application must be submitted by the fifteenth.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grazie. Che cosa significa presentare, in questo caso?
lines.en: Thank you. What does presentare mean, in this case?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vuol dire consegnare il modulo qui allo sportello.
lines.en: It means handing in the form here at the counter.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ho capito. E il nome dell'ufficio come si scrive?
lines.en: I see. And how do you spell the name of the office?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Glielo scrivo io sul foglio, così è più semplice.
lines.en: I will write it down for you on the sheet, that way it is simpler.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La ringrazio, adesso è tutto chiaro.
lines.en: Thank you, now it is all clear.

days.title: Aprire un conto in banca
days.objective: Open a current account and handle the basic operations of paying and transferring money.
days.paretoFocus: A local account is needed for rent, salary and bills, and the words bonifico, IBAN and canone come back every single month.
drills: List the documents you would take to open an account.
Explain in Italian how you would pay your rent every month.
Ask about the monthly cost of a card and an account.

items: What is a bonifico?
What does the IBAN identify?
How do you ask what the account costs per year?

activities.title: Il conto corrente
activities.intro: The everyday account is il conto corrente. A bank transfer is un bonifico, and it is made to an IBAN, the long code that identifies an account. Setting up an automatic payment for a bill is called una domiciliazione.

items.hr: Vorrei aprire un conto corrente.
items.en: I would like to open a current account.

items.hr: Servono un documento d'identità e il codice fiscale.
items.en: An identity document and the tax code are needed.

items.hr: Ogni mese faccio un bonifico per l'affitto.
items.en: Every month I make a transfer for the rent.
items.note: fare un bonifico = to make a bank transfer

items.hr: Mi può dare il suo IBAN, per favore?
items.en: Can you give me your IBAN, please?

items.hr: Ho chiesto la domiciliazione delle bollette.
items.en: I asked for the bills to be paid automatically from the account.

activities.title: Operazioni quotidiane
activities.intro: Practise the words for accounts, transfers and card payments.

questions.prompt: Un bonifico is:
options: a transfer of money from one account to another
a cash withdrawal at a machine
the monthly cost of the account
a receipt for a card payment
questions.answer: a transfer of money from one account to another
questions.explanation: Il bonifico moves money between accounts, and it needs the IBAN of the person receiving it.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the combined pronouns correctly?
options: Il codice IBAN glielo mando domani.
Il codice IBAN gli lo mando domani.
Il codice IBAN lo gli mando domani.
Il codice IBAN le lo mando domani.
questions.answer: Il codice IBAN glielo mando domani.
questions.explanation: Gli plus lo becomes glielo, written as one word before the verb.

questions.prompt: Complete with the long code that identifies a bank account: mi manda il suo ___ , per favore?
questions.explanation: The IBAN identifies the individual account and is required for any transfer.

questions.prompt: Complete with the type of everyday account: vorrei aprire un conto ___ .
questions.explanation: Il conto corrente is the ordinary account used for daily payments.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Every month I make a transfer for the rent.'
questions.explanation: Ogni is always followed by a singular noun, so ogni mese and never ogni mesi.

activities.title: Allo sportello della banca
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You open an account and ask about the costs and the card.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, si accomodi. Che operazione desidera fare?
lines.en: Good morning, take a seat. What would you like to do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vorrei aprire un conto corrente. Che documenti servono?
lines.en: I would like to open a current account. What documents are needed?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Un documento d'identità valido e il codice fiscale.
lines.en: A valid identity document and the tax code.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Li ho con me. Il conto ha un canone mensile?
lines.en: I have them with me. Does the account have a monthly fee?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, pochi euro al mese, con la carta compresa.
lines.en: Yes, a few euros a month, with the card included.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Con questo conto posso fare i bonifici dal telefono?
lines.en: With this account can I make transfers from my phone?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo, e può anche attivare la domiciliazione delle bollette.
lines.en: Certainly, and you can also set up automatic payment of the bills.

days.title: Il servizio sanitario e la tessera
days.objective: Register with the health service, choose a family doctor and understand what the tessera sanitaria is for.
days.paretoFocus: Registering once gives you a doctor, cheaper prescriptions and hospital care, and the card is asked for at every pharmacy and clinic counter.
drills: Explain in Italian the steps you must follow to sign up for public healthcare.
Name three situations in which you would show your tessera sanitaria.
Ask how to choose or change your family doctor.

items: Who is il medico di base?
What is il ticket?
Where do you have to show the tessera sanitaria?

activities.title: Iscriversi al servizio sanitario
activities.intro: When you register with the national health service you choose a medico di base, a family doctor who is your first contact for everything. You receive a tessera sanitaria, the card you show at the pharmacy, the clinic and the hospital. Il ticket is the share of the cost that the patient pays for some visits and tests.

items.hr: Mi sono iscritto al servizio sanitario del mio comune.
items.en: I registered with the health service of my town.

items.hr: Ho scelto un medico di base vicino a casa.
items.en: I chose a family doctor near my home.
items.note: il medico di base = il medico di famiglia

items.hr: La tessera sanitaria serve in farmacia e in ospedale.
items.en: The health card is needed at the pharmacy and at the hospital.

items.hr: Per gli esami del sangue ho pagato il ticket.
items.en: For the blood tests I paid the patient contribution.
items.note: il ticket = the part of the cost paid by the patient

items.hr: Il medico di base scrive le ricette e manda dallo specialista.
items.en: The family doctor writes prescriptions and refers you to the specialist.

activities.title: Come funziona la sanità
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of the health service and reflexive verbs in the past.

questions.prompt: Il medico di base is the doctor who:
options: is your first contact and refers you to specialists
works only in the emergency department
visits patients only at the hospital
questions.answer: is your first contact and refers you to specialists
questions.explanation: The family doctor is the entry point of the system: prescriptions, certificates and referrals all start there.

questions.prompt: Il ticket is:
options: the amount the patient pays towards a visit or a test
the appointment slip for the specialist
the queue number at the counter
the card that proves you are registered
questions.answer: the amount the patient pays towards a visit or a test
questions.explanation: Care is largely public, but for some services the patient pays a contribution called il ticket.

questions.prompt: Complete with the card you show at the pharmacy: ho dimenticato a casa la ___ sanitaria.
questions.explanation: La tessera sanitaria carries your tax code and proves that you are registered.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct auxiliary for a reflexive verb: mi ___ iscritto al servizio sanitario il mese scorso.
questions.explanation: Reflexive verbs always take essere in the passato prossimo, and the participle agrees with the subject.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I chose a family doctor near my home.'
questions.explanation: Scegliere has the irregular participle scelto, used here with avere.

activities.title: Scegliere il medico
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. At the health service counter you register and choose a family doctor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno. È qui per l'iscrizione al servizio sanitario?
lines.en: Good morning. Are you here to register with the health service?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì. Ho la residenza da questo mese e vorrei iscrivermi.
lines.en: Yes. I have been registered as resident since this month and I would like to sign up.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bene. Deve anche scegliere un medico di base dalla lista.
lines.en: Good. You also have to choose a family doctor from the list.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne vorrei uno vicino a casa mia, se è possibile.
lines.en: I would like one near my home, if that is possible.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo, questi tre ricevono nella sua zona.
lines.en: Of course, these three see patients in your area.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prendo il primo. Quando ricevo la tessera sanitaria?
lines.en: I will take the first one. When do I get the health card?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le arriva per posta. Intanto le do questo foglio provvisorio.
lines.en: It will arrive by post. In the meantime I will give you this temporary sheet.

days.title: Dal medico, descrivere i sintomi
days.objective: Describe symptoms accurately to a doctor, including where it hurts and since when.
days.paretoFocus: Doctors ask the same three questions everywhere: what hurts, since when, and how much, so preparing those answers gets you understood on the worst day.
drills: Describe three different symptoms in Italian, each with a duration.
Say what you did before the symptoms started, using the past.
Practise answering the question how long has this been going on.

items: How do you say my throat hurts?
How do you say I have had a temperature since Monday?
Which verb changes form with plural body parts?

activities.title: Che cosa si sente?
activities.intro: The main structure is mi fa male plus a singular body part, and mi fanno male with a plural one. Say how long with da plus a time. Use avere for states like avere la febbre and sentirsi for how you feel overall.

items.hr: Mi fa male la gola da tre giorni.
items.en: My throat has been hurting for three days.
items.note: singular part, so fa male

items.hr: Mi fanno male le orecchie soprattutto la notte.
items.en: My ears hurt especially at night.
items.note: plural part, so fanno male

items.hr: Ho la febbre alta e la tosse.
items.en: I have a high temperature and a cough.

items.hr: Mi sento debole e non riesco a dormire.
items.en: I feel weak and I cannot sleep.
items.note: riuscire a = to manage to

items.hr: Prima stavo bene, poi sono peggiorato all'improvviso.
items.en: Before I was fine, then I got worse all of a sudden.
items.note: imperfetto for the background, passato prossimo for the change

activities.title: Dove le fa male?
activities.intro: Practise fare male with singular and plural parts and the past tenses in a story.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence for aching legs:
questions.explanation: Fare male agrees with the body part, which is the subject, so a plural part takes fanno.

questions.prompt: Which pair of tenses is right? Prima ___ bene, poi ___ male all'improvviso.
questions.explanation: The imperfetto sets the background state and the passato prossimo marks the sudden change.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb that agrees with the body part: mi ___ male la schiena da una settimana.
questions.explanation: La schiena is singular, so the verb is fa male.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for a high body temperature: ho la ___ da ieri sera.
questions.explanation: Italian uses avere with febbre, tosse and mal di testa.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'My throat has been hurting for three days.'
questions.explanation: Italian keeps the present tense with da when the pain is still going on.

activities.title: La visita dal medico
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You describe your symptoms and answer the doctor's questions about when they started.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, si accomodi. Mi dica, che cosa si sente?
lines.en: Good morning, take a seat. Tell me, what is wrong?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi fa male la gola e ho la febbre da tre giorni.
lines.en: My throat hurts and I have had a temperature for three days.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quanto le è salita la febbre, più o meno?
lines.en: How high has the temperature gone, more or less?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ieri sera trentotto e mezzo. Mi sento anche molto debole.
lines.en: Last night thirty-eight and a half. I also feel very weak.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha anche la tosse? Prima stava bene?
lines.en: Do you also have a cough? Were you well before?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, un po' di tosse. Stavo bene, poi sono peggiorato all'improvviso.
lines.en: Yes, a bit of a cough. I was fine, then I got worse all of a sudden.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Adesso la visito. Le scrivo una ricetta e beva molta acqua.
lines.en: I will examine you now. I will write you a prescription and drink plenty of water.

days.title: In farmacia
days.objective: Buy medicine with or without a prescription and understand the dosage instructions.
days.paretoFocus: The pharmacist is the fastest medical advice available in Italy, and knowing how to describe a small problem saves a doctor's appointment.
drills: Ask for something for three different small ailments.
Repeat back a dosage instruction to check you understood it.
Ask whether a medicine needs a prescription.

items: What is un farmaco da banco?
How do you ask how many times a day to take something?
How do you say after meals?

activities.title: Qualcosa per il mal di gola
activities.intro: Ask with qualcosa per plus the problem. Medicines that need no prescription are farmaci da banco; the others need una ricetta. The dosage is given as a number of times a day, and prima or dopo i pasti, before or after meals.

items.hr: Vorrei qualcosa per il mal di gola.
items.en: I would like something for a sore throat.

items.hr: Questo è un farmaco da banco, non serve la ricetta.
items.en: This is an over the counter medicine, no prescription is needed.
items.note: da banco = sold without prescription

items.hr: Ne prenda una compressa tre volte al giorno.
items.en: Take one tablet three times a day.
items.note: ne prenda = formal imperative with ne

items.hr: Lo sciroppo si prende dopo i pasti.
items.en: The syrup is taken after meals.

items.hr: Legga il foglio illustrativo prima di iniziare.
items.en: Read the information leaflet before starting.
items.note: il foglio illustrativo = the package insert

activities.title: Le istruzioni del farmacista
activities.intro: Practise asking for medicine and reading dosage instructions.

questions.prompt: Un farmaco da banco is a medicine that:
options: can be bought without a prescription
is paid for entirely by the health service
can only be taken in hospital
is prepared by the pharmacist on the spot
questions.answer: can be bought without a prescription
questions.explanation: Da banco means over the counter, so no ricetta from the doctor is required.

questions.prompt: Dopo i pasti tells you to take the medicine:
questions.explanation: Il pasto is a meal; prima dei pasti would be before meals.

questions.prompt: Complete the request: vorrei ___ per il mal di testa.
questions.explanation: Qualcosa per plus the ailment is the standard way to ask a pharmacist for advice.

questions.prompt: Complete with the leaflet inside the box: prima di prendere il farmaco legga il foglio ___ .
questions.explanation: Il foglio illustrativo is the printed insert with the dosage and side effects.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Take one tablet three times a day.'
questions.explanation: With the formal imperative the pronoun ne goes before the verb, not attached to it.

activities.title: Al banco della farmacia
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You describe a small problem and check the dosage before leaving.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buonasera, mi dica pure.
lines.en: Good evening, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vorrei qualcosa per la tosse, ma senza ricetta.
lines.en: I would like something for a cough, but without a prescription.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Questo sciroppo va bene, è un farmaco da banco.
lines.en: This syrup is fine, it is an over the counter medicine.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quante volte al giorno lo devo prendere?
lines.en: How many times a day do I have to take it?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tre volte al giorno, dopo i pasti, per non più di cinque giorni.
lines.en: Three times a day, after meals, for no more than five days.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Scusi, non ho capito: cinque giorni al massimo?
lines.en: Sorry, I did not catch that: five days at most?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Esatto. Se la tosse continua, vada dal medico di base.
lines.en: Exactly. If the cough continues, go to your family doctor.

days.title: Dal dentista e le urgenze
days.objective: Book a dental appointment, describe tooth pain and ask to be seen urgently.
days.paretoFocus: Dental care is mostly private in Italy and toothache does not wait, so being able to ask for an urgent slot and a quote is worth a great deal.
drills: Describe tooth pain in three ways: constant, when eating, when drinking cold.
Ask for the first free slot and say you are in pain.
Ask for a written quote before treatment.

items: How do you say it is an emergency?
What is un preventivo?
How do you ask for the first available appointment?

activities.title: Un dolore ai denti
activities.intro: Say which tooth and when it hurts. Use è un'urgenza to ask for a quick slot and la prima disponibilità for the first free appointment. Before private treatment always ask for un preventivo, a written estimate.

items.hr: Mi fa malissimo un dente in fondo a destra.
items.en: A tooth at the back on the right hurts terribly.
items.note: malissimo = the absolute superlative of male

items.hr: Il dolore aumenta quando bevo qualcosa di freddo.
items.en: The pain gets worse when I drink something cold.

items.hr: È un'urgenza, non riesco a masticare.
items.en: It is an emergency, I cannot chew.
items.note: un'urgenza with the apostrophe, feminine

items.hr: Mi può dare la prima disponibilità, anche presto la mattina?
items.en: Can you give me the first available slot, even early in the morning?

items.hr: Prima di iniziare la cura vorrei un preventivo scritto.
items.en: Before starting the treatment I would like a written estimate.

activities.title: Chiedere un posto urgente
activities.intro: Practise describing dental pain and asking for an urgent appointment.

questions.prompt: Un preventivo is:
options: a written estimate of the cost
a receipt for a payment already made
a reminder of your next appointment
questions.answer: a written estimate of the cost
questions.explanation: Il preventivo lists the planned treatment and its price before the work begins.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct absolute superlative of male:
questions.explanation: The ending issimo attaches to the adverb, giving malissimo, benissimo, tantissimo.

questions.prompt: Complete with the feminine noun for an emergency, with the correct apostrophe: è un' ___ , non riesco a masticare.
questions.explanation: Urgenza is feminine, so the article una becomes un' before the vowel.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for the earliest free slot: mi dia la prima ___ , per favore.
questions.explanation: La prima disponibilità is the fixed phrase for the first free appointment.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The pain gets worse when I drink something cold.'
questions.explanation: Qualcosa takes di before an adjective, as in qualcosa di caldo, qualcosa di buono.

activities.title: Chiamata allo studio dentistico
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are in pain and you push for an appointment today, then ask about the cost.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Studio dentistico, buongiorno. Mi dica.
lines.en: Dental practice, good morning. Go ahead.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno. Mi fa malissimo un dente da ieri notte.
lines.en: Good morning. A tooth has been hurting terribly since last night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi dispiace. Il primo posto libero sarebbe martedì.
lines.en: I am sorry. The first free slot would be Tuesday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È un'urgenza, non riesco a mangiare. Non c'è niente prima?
lines.en: It is an emergency, I cannot eat. Is there nothing sooner?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Aspetti un attimo. Potremmo vederla oggi alle diciotto.
lines.en: Wait a moment. We could see you today at six.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfetto, ci sono. Quanto costa la prima visita?
lines.en: Perfect, I will be there. How much does the first visit cost?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La visita ha un costo fisso, poi le facciamo un preventivo.
lines.en: The visit has a fixed cost, then we will give you an estimate.

days.title: Il pronto soccorso
days.objective: Explain what happened at the emergency department and understand the colour code and the waiting.
days.paretoFocus: In an emergency you must narrate a past event under pressure, and knowing the triage system explains why you wait while others go first.
drills: Narrate a small accident in three past sentences.
Explain your codice colore to someone waiting with you.
Ask how long the wait is likely to be.

items: What does il triage decide?
Which colour means the least urgent case?
Say I fell and hurt my wrist.

activities.title: Al pronto soccorso
activities.intro: At the emergency department a nurse does il triage and assigns a colour code: the more serious the case, the sooner you are seen, so a long wait usually means your case is not dangerous. Tell the story with the passato prossimo for the events and the imperfetto for the background.

items.hr: Sono caduto per strada e mi sono fatto male al polso.
items.en: I fell in the street and hurt my wrist.
items.note: cadere and farsi male both take essere

items.hr: Pioveva e la strada era bagnata.
items.en: It was raining and the road was wet.
items.note: imperfetto for the background

items.hr: All'accettazione ho dato la tessera sanitaria.
items.en: At the reception desk I handed over my health card.

items.hr: Mi hanno assegnato un codice non urgente.
items.en: They gave me a non urgent code.

items.hr: L'attesa può essere lunga, perché passano prima i casi gravi.
items.en: The wait can be long, because serious cases go first.
items.note: perché with the acute accent

activities.title: Raccontare l'incidente
activities.intro: Practise narrating an accident with the two past tenses and the words of the emergency department.

questions.prompt: You waited four hours while others went in first. The most likely reason is:
options: your case was judged less urgent at triage
you did not pay the ticket
your family doctor did not call ahead
questions.answer: your case was judged less urgent at triage
questions.explanation: The order is set by the colour code assigned at triage, not by the time of arrival.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the two past tenses correctly?
questions.explanation: The imperfetto describes the ongoing weather and the passato prossimo reports the single event.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct auxiliary: mi ___ fatto male al ginocchio.
questions.explanation: Farsi male is reflexive, so it takes essere in the passato prossimo.

questions.prompt: Complete with the desk where you register on arrival: ho consegnato i documenti all' ___ .
questions.explanation: L'accettazione is the reception desk that opens your file.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I fell in the street and hurt my wrist.'
questions.explanation: Both verbs take essere, and the participles agree with the subject.

activities.title: Al triage
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A nurse asks what happened and explains the wait.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buonasera. Mi racconti che cosa è successo.
lines.en: Good evening. Tell me what happened.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono caduto per strada e mi sono fatto male al polso.
lines.en: I fell in the street and hurt my wrist.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quando è successo? Ha battuto la testa?
lines.en: When did it happen? Did you hit your head?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un'ora fa. No, la testa no, pioveva e sono scivolato.
lines.en: An hour ago. No, not my head, it was raining and I slipped.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bene. Le assegno un codice non urgente, dovrà aspettare.
lines.en: Good. I am giving you a non urgent code, you will have to wait.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quanto tempo, più o meno? Il polso è molto gonfio.
lines.en: How long, more or less? The wrist is very swollen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Circa due ore. Se peggiora, torni subito da me.
lines.en: About two hours. If it gets worse, come straight back to me.

days.title: Dare un consiglio
days.objective: Give advice to a friend or a colleague without sounding like you are giving orders.
days.paretoFocus: Advice is the most frequent thing you do with the conditional, and choosing the right form decides whether you sound helpful or bossy.
drills: Give three pieces of advice to someone who has a bad cough.
Rewrite one order as advice using dovresti.
Advise a colleague about a work problem, using Lei.

items: What does ti conviene mean?
Which is softer, dovresti or devi?
Give one piece of advice in the Lei form.

activities.title: Se posso darti un consiglio
activities.intro: The conditional of dovere gives dovresti and dovrebbe, the standard way to advise. Ti conviene means it is in your interest, and io al posto tuo means if I were you. Perché non plus the present turns advice into a friendly suggestion.

items.hr: Dovresti riposare almeno due giorni.
items.en: You should rest for at least two days.
items.note: dovresti = tu, dovrebbe = Lei

items.hr: Ti conviene arrivare mezz'ora prima.
items.en: It is in your interest to arrive half an hour early.
items.note: convenire = to be worth it for someone

items.hr: Io al posto tuo chiederei un preventivo.
items.en: If I were you I would ask for an estimate.
items.note: al posto tuo plus the conditional

items.hr: Perché non ne parli direttamente con lui?
items.en: Why not talk to him about it directly?

items.hr: Le consiglierei di portare tutti i documenti.
items.en: I would advise you to bring all the documents.
items.note: consigliare a qualcuno di plus infinitive, formal Le

activities.title: Consigli, non ordini
activities.intro: Practise turning instructions into advice with the conditional.

questions.prompt: Which sentence sounds like advice rather than an order?
options: Dovresti parlare con il tuo capo.
Devi parlare con il tuo capo.
questions.answer: Dovresti parlare con il tuo capo.
questions.explanation: The conditional dovresti leaves the decision with the other person, while devi and the imperative do not.

questions.prompt: Ti conviene partire presto means:
options: it is in your interest to leave early
you are forced to leave early
you would like to leave early
questions.answer: it is in your interest to leave early
questions.explanation: Convenire expresses advantage, not obligation, and it agrees with the thing that is advantageous.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conditional of dovere (tu form): ___ riposare almeno due giorni.
questions.explanation: The conditional of dovere is dovrei, dovresti, dovrebbe, with a double r.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite formal advice: le ___ di portare tutti i documenti.
questions.explanation: Consigliare keeps the i of the stem only once in the conditional, giving consiglierei.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'If I were you I would ask for an estimate.'
questions.explanation: Al posto tuo avoids a hypothetical clause and works with a simple conditional.

activities.title: Un amico ti chiede aiuto
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend has a problem with a landlord and you give three pieces of advice.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il proprietario non risponde e la caldaia è ancora rotta.
lines.en: The landlord is not answering and the boiler is still broken.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dovresti scrivergli un messaggio con la data del guasto.
lines.en: You should write him a message with the date of the fault.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gliel'ho già detto due volte al telefono.
lines.en: I have already told him twice on the phone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora ti conviene mettere tutto per iscritto.
lines.en: Then it is in your interest to put everything in writing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se continua a non fare niente?
lines.en: And if he still does nothing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Io al posto tuo chiederei un preventivo a un tecnico.
lines.en: If I were you I would ask a technician for an estimate.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai ragione, così ho un documento in mano.
lines.en: You are right, that way I have a document in my hand.

days.title: Leggere un annuncio di lavoro
days.objective: Understand a job advert: the tasks, the requirements and how to apply.
days.paretoFocus: Job adverts repeat the same headings, so once you can read si richiede and mansioni you can scan dozens of adverts quickly.
drills: Write the three headings of a typical job advert and one line under each.
List four requirements you personally meet, in Italian.
Say which type of contract an advert offers.

items: What are le mansioni?
What does si richiede introduce?
How do you say previous experience is required?

activities.title: Requisiti e mansioni
activities.intro: Adverts are written impersonally: si richiede introduces what they want from you and si offre what they give. Le mansioni are the duties of the job and i requisiti the requirements. The advert usually ends with how to send your CV.

items.hr: Si richiede esperienza di almeno un anno.
items.en: At least one year of experience is required.
items.note: si richiede plus singular, si richiedono plus plural

items.hr: Le mansioni comprendono l'accoglienza dei clienti.
items.en: The duties include welcoming customers.

items.hr: Si offre un contratto a tempo determinato di sei mesi.
items.en: A fixed term contract of six months is offered.

items.hr: È gradita la conoscenza di una seconda lingua.
items.en: Knowledge of a second language is welcome.
items.note: gradito = appreciated but not compulsory

items.hr: Per candidarsi occorre inviare il curriculum aggiornato.
items.en: To apply you need to send an updated CV.
items.note: candidarsi = to apply for a post

activities.title: Capire l'offerta
activities.intro: Practise the impersonal si and the standard headings of a job advert.

questions.prompt: In an advert, le mansioni tell you:
options: what you will actually do in the job
how much you will be paid
who else works in the team
questions.answer: what you will actually do in the job
questions.explanation: Le mansioni are the duties; the requirements are i requisiti and the pay is la retribuzione.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct impersonal form: si ___ due anni di esperienza.
questions.explanation: With the impersonal si the verb agrees with what follows, and due anni is plural.

questions.prompt: Complete with the reflexive verb meaning to apply for a job: per ___ occorre inviare il curriculum.
questions.explanation: Candidarsi is reflexive, so after a preposition the infinitive keeps its pronoun.

questions.prompt: Complete with the adjective describing something appreciated but not compulsory: è ___ la conoscenza di una seconda lingua.
questions.explanation: Gradito agrees with la conoscenza, which is feminine singular.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'A fixed term contract of six months is offered.'
questions.explanation: A tempo determinato is the fixed phrase for a contract with an end date.

activities.title: Ho visto un annuncio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You discuss a job advert with a friend and work out whether you meet the requirements.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho visto un annuncio interessante per un lavoro in negozio.
lines.en: I saw an interesting advert for a job in a shop.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Che cosa si richiede? Serve esperienza?
lines.en: What is required? Is experience needed?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, almeno un anno, e la conoscenza di una seconda lingua è gradita.
lines.en: Yes, at least a year, and knowledge of a second language is welcome.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora sono a posto. E quali sono le mansioni?
lines.en: Then I am fine. And what are the duties?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Accoglienza dei clienti e gestione della cassa.
lines.en: Welcoming customers and handling the till.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Che contratto offrono? A tempo determinato?
lines.en: What contract do they offer? Fixed term?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sei mesi, poi si vede. Per candidarti devi mandare il curriculum.
lines.en: Six months, then we will see. To apply you have to send your CV.

days.title: Il curriculum vitae
days.objective: Write the sections of an Italian CV and describe your experience in short, clear lines.
days.paretoFocus: An Italian CV follows a fixed order of sections, and using the standard headings makes you look like someone who knows how things work here.
drills: Write the five section headings of a CV in Italian.
Describe two past jobs in one line each, with dates.
Rate your own languages using the usual wording.

items: What goes under dati personali?
How do you write the dates of a past job?
Which line about data goes at the end of a CV?

activities.title: Le sezioni del curriculum
activities.intro: An Italian CV starts with dati personali, then esperienze lavorative in reverse order, then istruzione e formazione, then competenze and lingue. Job entries are written as dates plus role plus place. At the bottom Italians add a short line authorising the use of their personal data.

items.hr: Nei dati personali metto nome, indirizzo e recapiti.
items.en: In the personal details I put my name, address and contact details.
items.note: i recapiti = phone and email

items.hr: Le esperienze lavorative vanno dalla più recente alla più vecchia.
items.en: Work experience goes from the most recent to the oldest.

items.hr: Dal duemilaventuno al duemilaventitré ho lavorato come cameriere.
items.en: From twenty twenty one to twenty twenty three I worked as a waiter.
items.note: lavorare come plus job, with no article

items.hr: Tra le competenze ho scritto che uso bene il computer.
items.en: Among my skills I wrote that I use a computer well.
