items.hr: In fondo autorizzo il trattamento dei dati personali.
items.en: At the bottom I authorise the processing of my personal data.

activities.title: Scrivere il curriculum
activities.intro: Practise the sections of a CV and the way jobs and dates are written.

questions.prompt: In an Italian CV, work experience is normally listed:
options: starting from the most recent job
starting from the first job you ever had
only for the last twelve months
questions.answer: starting from the most recent job
questions.explanation: Reverse chronological order is the standard, so the reader sees your current situation first.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct way to name a job you did:
questions.explanation: After come the name of a job takes no article, like faccio l'insegnante but lavoro come insegnante.

questions.prompt: Complete with the section that holds your name and address: nei dati ___ metto anche i recapiti.
questions.explanation: I dati personali is the first heading of the CV and covers your identity and contacts.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for your phone number and email: in alto scrivo i miei ___ .
questions.explanation: I recapiti are the ways of reaching you, used on forms and CVs alike.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Work experience goes from the most recent to the oldest.'
questions.explanation: The relative superlative uses the definite article inside the preposition: dalla più, alla più.

activities.title: Rivedere il curriculum
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend checks your CV and you explain the choices you made.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fammi vedere. Hai messo prima l'esperienza più vecchia?
lines.en: Let me see. Did you put the oldest experience first?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, ho messo prima la più recente, come si usa qui.
lines.en: No, I put the most recent one first, as is usual here.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bene. E qui che cosa facevi esattamente?
lines.en: Good. And here what exactly were you doing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ho lavorato come cameriere per due anni in un ristorante.
lines.en: I worked as a waiter for two years in a restaurant.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Aggiungerei anche le lingue, sono importanti.
lines.en: I would also add languages, they are important.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le ho scritte sotto le competenze, con il livello accanto.
lines.en: I wrote them under skills, with the level next to them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfetto. Non dimenticare la frase sul trattamento dei dati.
lines.en: Perfect. Do not forget the line about the processing of data.

days.title: La lettera di presentazione
days.objective: Write a short covering letter with the right opening, body and closing formulas.
days.paretoFocus: Italian written formality is formulaic, so four fixed phrases carry a whole letter and also work for emails to any office.
drills: Write the opening and closing lines of a formal letter.
Write two sentences saying why you are the right person for a job.
Rewrite an informal message as a formal one.

items: How do you open a letter to someone whose name you do not know?
How do you say you attach your CV?
Which closing formula is the safest?

activities.title: Gentile responsabile
activities.intro: Open with Gentile plus a role, or Spettabile plus an organisation. Say which post you are writing about, give two reasons in your favour, mention what you attach, and close with a fixed formula. Everything is in the Lei form or in the impersonal.

items.hr: Gentile responsabile, le scrivo in merito all'annuncio pubblicato.
items.en: Dear manager, I am writing to you regarding the advert published.
items.note: in merito a = regarding

items.hr: Mi permetto di propormi per la posizione di addetto alle vendite.
items.en: I would like to put myself forward for the position of sales assistant.
items.note: mi permetto di is a polite formal opener

items.hr: Ho maturato tre anni di esperienza nel settore.
items.en: I have gained three years of experience in the sector.
items.note: maturare esperienza is the standard collocation

items.hr: In allegato invio il mio curriculum aggiornato.
items.en: Attached I am sending my updated CV.

items.hr: Resto a disposizione per un colloquio. Distinti saluti.
items.en: I remain available for an interview. Kind regards.
items.note: Distinti saluti is the safe formal closing

activities.title: Formule della lettera
activities.intro: Practise the fixed phrases that hold a formal letter together.

questions.prompt: You do not know the name of the person reading. The best opening is:
questions.explanation: Gentile plus a role is neutral and formal; caro and ciao belong to informal writing.

questions.prompt: Which closing is right for a job application?
questions.explanation: Distinti saluti and Cordiali saluti are the formal closings; the others are for friends.

questions.prompt: Complete the phrase introducing what you are sending with the letter: in ___ invio il mio curriculum.
questions.explanation: In allegato means attached, and l'allegato is the attachment itself.

questions.prompt: Complete the formal reference to the advert: le scrivo in ___ all'annuncio pubblicato ieri.
questions.explanation: In merito a is a formal equivalent of riguardo a, meaning about or concerning.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I remain available for an interview.'
questions.explanation: Restare a disposizione is the fixed formula that signals the end of a formal letter.

activities.title: Controlliamo la lettera
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague reads your covering letter and suggests changes to the formal register.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai iniziato con Ciao. Per una candidatura non va bene.
lines.en: You started with Ciao. For an application that is not right.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hai ragione. Scrivo Gentile responsabile, allora.
lines.en: You are right. I will write Gentile responsabile, then.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meglio. E qui dici solo che cerchi lavoro.
lines.en: Better. And here you only say that you are looking for work.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aggiungo che ho maturato tre anni di esperienza nel settore.
lines.en: I will add that I have gained three years of experience in the sector.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ottimo. Dici anche che mandi il curriculum?
lines.en: Excellent. Do you also say that you are sending the CV?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, scrivo che in allegato invio il curriculum aggiornato.
lines.en: Yes, I write that attached I am sending my updated CV.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alla fine metti Distinti saluti e sei a posto.
lines.en: At the end put Distinti saluti and you are done.

days.title: Il colloquio di lavoro
days.objective: Answer the standard interview questions about yourself, your strengths and your availability.
days.paretoFocus: Interviews reuse five or six questions, so preparing those answers once means you can walk into any interview with something ready to say.
drills: Answer the question tell me about yourself in four sentences.
Name two strengths and one weakness, each with an example.
State your availability to start and your working hours.

items: How do you say your strong points?
How do you say you are available from the first of the month?
Which pronoun do you use with the interviewer?

activities.title: Mi parli di lei
activities.intro: The interviewer uses Lei and so do you. The classic questions are mi parli di lei, quali sono i suoi punti di forza and quando è disponibile. Answer with concrete facts, not adjectives alone, and always ask one question at the end.

items.hr: Mi occupo di assistenza clienti da tre anni.
items.en: I have been working in customer service for three years.
items.note: occuparsi di = to deal with, to work in

items.hr: I miei punti di forza sono la puntualità e la calma.
items.en: My strong points are punctuality and calmness.

items.hr: A volte sono troppo preciso, ma sto imparando a essere più rapido.
items.en: Sometimes I am too precise, but I am learning to be quicker.

items.hr: Sono disponibile a partire dal primo del mese.
items.en: I am available starting from the first of the month.
items.note: a partire da = starting from

items.hr: Posso farle una domanda sull'orario?
items.en: May I ask you a question about the working hours?

activities.title: Rispondere bene
activities.intro: Practise interview answers in the formal register.

questions.prompt: The interviewer says mi parli di lei. They want you to:
options: explain what you know about them
questions.explanation: It is an invitation to summarise your professional profile, not your private life.

questions.prompt: Which answer keeps the formal register with the interviewer?
questions.explanation: Le is the formal indirect pronoun, attached to the infinitive as farle.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for the qualities you are best at: i miei punti di ___ sono la puntualità e la calma.
questions.explanation: I punti di forza are your strengths, and i punti deboli are your weaknesses.

questions.prompt: Complete with the reflexive verb meaning to work in a field: mi ___ di assistenza clienti da tre anni.
questions.explanation: Occuparsi di is followed directly by the area of work, without an article in this use.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am available starting from the first of the month.'
questions.explanation: Dates of the month use the ordinal only for the first: il primo, then il due, il tre.

activities.title: Il primo colloquio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You answer the standard questions and finish by asking one of your own, all in the Lei form.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Si accomodi. Allora, mi parli un po' di lei.
lines.en: Take a seat. So, tell me a little about yourself.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi occupo di assistenza clienti da tre anni, soprattutto al telefono.
lines.en: I have been working in customer service for three years, mostly on the phone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quali sono i suoi punti di forza?
lines.en: What are your strong points?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La puntualità e la calma anche quando il cliente è arrabbiato.
lines.en: Punctuality and staying calm even when the customer is angry.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bene. Da quando sarebbe disponibile?
lines.en: Good. From when would you be available?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A partire dal primo del mese prossimo, senza problemi.
lines.en: Starting from the first of next month, without any problem.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfetto. Ha qualche domanda per noi?
lines.en: Perfect. Do you have any questions for us?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, posso farle una domanda sull'orario di lavoro?
lines.en: Yes, may I ask you a question about the working hours?

days.title: Il contratto e l'orario di lavoro
days.objective: Read the key points of an employment contract: type, trial period, hours, holidays and the extra month of pay.
days.paretoFocus: The difference between a fixed term and a permanent contract decides your housing, your loans and your peace of mind, and the tredicesima surprises every newcomer.
drills: Explain the difference between the two contract types in Italian.
Say your weekly hours and your holiday entitlement.
Ask three questions about a contract before signing it.

items: What is il periodo di prova?
What is la tredicesima?
How do you say forty hours a week?

activities.title: Determinato o indeterminato
activities.intro: A contratto a tempo determinato has an end date, while a tempo indeterminato does not. Most contracts open with un periodo di prova, during which either side can stop with very short notice. Employees earn paid holidays and usually a thirteenth monthly payment, la tredicesima, paid in December.

items.hr: Il contratto a tempo indeterminato non ha una scadenza.
items.en: The permanent contract has no end date.
items.note: la scadenza = the expiry date

items.hr: I primi tre mesi sono di prova.
items.en: The first three months are a trial period.

items.hr: L'orario è di quaranta ore alla settimana, dal lunedì al venerdì.
items.en: The hours are forty a week, from Monday to Friday.

items.hr: Ogni anno maturo circa ventisei giorni di ferie.
items.en: Every year I accrue about twenty six days of holiday.
items.note: maturare le ferie = to build up holiday entitlement

items.hr: A dicembre arriva la tredicesima, una mensilità in più.
items.en: In December the thirteenth salary arrives, one extra month of pay.

activities.title: Leggere il contratto
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of contracts, hours and pay.

questions.prompt: Un contratto a tempo indeterminato is one that:
options: is only for part time work
questions.explanation: Indeterminato means the end is not fixed; determinato means there is a date written in the contract.

questions.prompt: La tredicesima is:
options: an extra month of salary usually paid in December
the tax deducted from the salary
a bonus for working on public holidays
the thirteenth day of paid holiday
questions.answer: an extra month of salary usually paid in December
questions.explanation: Most employees receive thirteen monthly payments a year, and some sectors add a fourteenth in the summer.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for the initial trial months: i primi tre mesi sono di ___ .
questions.explanation: Il periodo di prova lets both sides end the contract quickly at the start.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for paid days off in the year: ogni anno maturo ventisei giorni di ___ .
questions.explanation: Le ferie are annual paid leave and the word exists only in the plural.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The hours are forty a week.'
questions.explanation: Italian uses essere di plus the quantity to state a fixed amount.

activities.title: Prima di firmare il contratto
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You go through the main points of a contract you have been offered.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ecco la proposta. Le spiego i punti principali.
lines.en: Here is the offer. I will explain the main points to you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grazie. È a tempo determinato o indeterminato?
lines.en: Thank you. Is it fixed term or permanent?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Determinato per un anno, poi valutiamo il rinnovo.
lines.en: Fixed term for one year, then we will consider renewal.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'è un periodo di prova? Di quanti mesi?
lines.en: Is there a trial period? How many months?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tre mesi. L'orario è di quaranta ore alla settimana.
lines.en: Three months. The hours are forty a week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E le ferie quante sono? La tredicesima è compresa?
lines.en: And how much holiday is there? Is the thirteenth salary included?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ventisei giorni di ferie e sì, la tredicesima arriva a dicembre.
lines.en: Twenty six days of holiday and yes, the thirteenth salary arrives in December.

days.title: I primi giorni al lavoro
days.objective: Get through your first days in a new job: introduce yourself, ask where things are and check what to do.
days.paretoFocus: In the first week you have a licence to ask anything, and using it well saves months of confusion later.
drills: Introduce yourself to a new team in two sentences.
Ask five practical questions about a workplace.
Repeat back an instruction to confirm you understood it.

items: How do you ask what you should do next?
How do you check that you understood an instruction?
How do you ask where the changing room is?

activities.title: Il primo giorno
activities.intro: On the first day you introduce yourself, get shown around and receive instructions. The useful moves are asking where something is, asking what to do next, and repeating an instruction back to confirm it. Start with Lei and switch to tu when your colleagues do.

items.hr: Buongiorno a tutti, sono la persona nuova del reparto.
items.en: Good morning everyone, I am the new person in the department.

items.hr: Scusi, dove si timbra all'entrata?
items.en: Excuse me, where do you clock in on arrival?
items.note: timbrare = to clock in with a badge

items.hr: Che cosa devo fare adesso?
items.en: What should I do now?

items.hr: Quindi devo controllare gli ordini e poi avvisarla, giusto?
items.en: So I have to check the orders and then let you know, is that right?
items.note: repeating back is the best way to avoid mistakes

items.hr: Se sbaglio qualcosa, me lo dica pure.
items.en: If I get something wrong, do tell me.
items.note: me lo dica = combined pronouns with the formal imperative

activities.title: Chiedere in ufficio
activities.intro: Practise the questions and pronoun combinations of a first week at work.

questions.prompt: The safest way to make sure you understood an instruction is:
options: repeat it back and ask giusto
write it down and ask nobody
questions.answer: repeat it back and ask giusto
questions.explanation: Repeating the instruction lets the other person correct you before the mistake is made.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct combined pronoun form with the formal imperative?
questions.explanation: Mi becomes me before another pronoun, and with the formal imperative the pair stays before the verb.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb for registering your arrival with a badge: dove si ___ all'entrata?
questions.explanation: Timbrare il cartellino is the everyday expression for clocking in and out.

questions.prompt: Complete the question about your next task: che cosa ___ fare adesso?
questions.explanation: Dovere in the io form asks about your own obligation, which is exactly what a new employee needs.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Good morning everyone, I am the new person in the department.'
questions.explanation: Nuovo after the noun means new in the sense of recently arrived.

activities.title: Il giro del reparto
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone shows you around on your first morning and gives you a first task.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Benvenuto. Le faccio vedere dove si timbra e dove si lascia la giacca.
lines.en: Welcome. I will show you where you clock in and where you leave your coat.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grazie. E il computer è già pronto per me?
lines.en: Thank you. And is the computer already set up for me?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, la password gliela do fra un attimo.
lines.en: Yes, I will give you the password in a moment.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfetto. Che cosa devo fare per prima cosa?
lines.en: Perfect. What should I do first?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Controlli gli ordini arrivati e mi avvisi quando ha finito.
lines.en: Check the orders that have arrived and let me know when you have finished.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quindi controllo gli ordini e poi la avviso, giusto?
lines.en: So I check the orders and then I let you know, is that right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Esatto. Se qualcosa non è chiaro, me lo chieda subito.
lines.en: Exactly. If something is not clear, ask me straight away.

days.title: Parlare con i colleghi
days.objective: Handle everyday exchanges with colleagues: small talk, offering help and asking for a favour.
days.paretoFocus: Most of your Italian at work happens in the corridor and at the coffee machine, and those short exchanges decide how included you feel.
drills: Offer help to a colleague in two different ways.
Ask a colleague for a small favour, politely.
Prepare three lines of coffee break small talk.

items: How do you offer to give someone a hand?
How do you ask a favour without sounding demanding?
When do you move from Lei to tu with a colleague?

activities.title: Alla macchinetta del caffè
activities.intro: Colleagues usually move to tu quickly, often after the phrase possiamo darci del tu. Offers use ti do una mano and requests use mi faresti un favore. Keep small talk short and about shared things: the weather, the work, the weekend.

items.hr: Possiamo darci del tu, se per te va bene.
items.en: We can use tu with each other, if that is fine with you.
items.note: darsi del tu = to switch to first name terms

items.hr: Vuoi una mano con quelle scatole?
items.en: Do you want a hand with those boxes?

items.hr: Mi faresti un favore? Devo uscire dieci minuti.
items.en: Would you do me a favour? I have to go out for ten minutes.

items.hr: Prendiamo un caffè insieme durante la pausa?
items.en: Shall we have a coffee together during the break?

items.hr: Ieri c'era parecchio lavoro, però siamo riusciti a finire.
items.en: Yesterday there was a lot of work, but we managed to finish.
items.note: però is a softer but than ma at the start of a clause

activities.title: Colleghi e favori
activities.intro: Practise offers, favours and the move from formal to informal.

questions.prompt: A colleague says possiamo darci del tu. This means:
options: let us use the informal tu from now on
let us split the task in two
questions.answer: let us use the informal tu from now on
questions.explanation: Darsi del tu is the standard phrase for agreeing to drop the formal Lei.

questions.prompt: Which is the politest way to ask a colleague for a favour?
questions.explanation: The conditional faresti turns the request into a question the other person can decline.

questions.prompt: Complete the offer of help: vuoi una ___ con quelle scatole?
questions.explanation: Dare una mano is the fixed expression for helping out, always singular here.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning to manage to do something: c'era molto lavoro, però siamo ___ a finire.
questions.explanation: Riuscire takes essere, so the participle agrees with the subject: siamo riusciti.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Shall we have a coffee together during the break?'
questions.explanation: The present tense of noi works as a suggestion, like shall we in English.

activities.title: Pausa di dieci minuti
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague suggests dropping the formal Lei, and you offer help and ask a favour.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Senta, possiamo darci del tu? Qui lo facciamo tutti.
lines.en: Listen, can we use tu with each other? Here everybody does.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Volentieri, è più semplice. Vuoi una mano con quelle scatole?
lines.en: Gladly, it is simpler. Do you want a hand with those boxes?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Grazie, sono pesanti. Le portiamo insieme di là.
lines.en: Thanks, they are heavy. Let us carry them over there together.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fatto. Senti, mi faresti un favore più tardi?
lines.en: Done. Listen, would you do me a favour later?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dimmi pure, se posso lo faccio.
lines.en: Go ahead, if I can I will do it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Devo uscire dieci minuti, rispondi tu al telefono?
lines.en: I have to go out for ten minutes, will you answer the phone?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nessun problema. Poi prendiamo un caffè insieme.
lines.en: No problem. Afterwards we will have a coffee together.

days.title: Chiedere le ferie
days.objective: Request holiday or a day of leave, in speech and in writing, and negotiate the dates.
days.paretoFocus: Holiday requests follow a fixed procedure everywhere, and asking in the right way with enough notice is what gets them approved.
drills: Ask for a week of holiday, giving dates and notice.
Ask for a single day of leave for a medical appointment.
Write a two line written request for time off.

items: Difference between le ferie and un permesso?
How do you say how many days you have left?
How do you offer to swap a shift?

activities.title: Vorrei chiedere le ferie
activities.intro: Le ferie are whole days of annual holiday, while un permesso is a few hours or a single day for something specific. The days you have not used yet are i giorni residui. Ask with the conditional, give the dates and say you have arranged cover.

items.hr: Vorrei chiedere una settimana di ferie a settembre.
items.en: I would like to ask for a week of holiday in September.

items.hr: Mi servirebbe un permesso di due ore giovedì.
items.en: I would need two hours of leave on Thursday.
items.note: il permesso = short leave, not a whole holiday

items.hr: Quanti giorni residui mi restano quest'anno?
items.en: How many unused days do I have left this year?

items.hr: Ho già parlato con un collega che mi sostituisce.
items.en: I have already spoken to a colleague who will cover for me.
items.note: sostituire = to stand in for

items.hr: Se quelle date non vanno bene, posso spostare la partenza.
items.en: If those dates do not work, I can move my departure.

activities.title: Ferie e permessi
activities.intro: Practise requesting time off and talking about the days you have left.

questions.prompt: You need two hours off for a medical appointment. You ask for:
questions.explanation: Un permesso covers a few hours or a single day; le ferie are your annual holiday days.

questions.prompt: I giorni residui are:
options: the holiday days you have not used yet
the days of the trial period
the public holidays of the year
questions.answer: the holiday days you have not used yet
questions.explanation: Residuo means left over, so i giorni residui is the balance still available to you.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning to stand in for someone: ho parlato con un collega che mi ___ .
questions.explanation: Sostituire adds isc in the present: sostituisco, sostituisci, sostituisce.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conditional of servire: mi ___ un permesso di due ore giovedì.
questions.explanation: Servire agrees with the thing needed, so un permesso singular gives servirebbe.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I would like to ask for a week of holiday in September.'
questions.explanation: Months take a: a settembre, a gennaio, with no article.

activities.title: Parlare con il responsabile
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You ask your manager for holiday and adjust the dates when there is a problem.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi dica pure, voleva parlarmi?
lines.en: Go ahead, you wanted to speak to me?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì. Vorrei chiedere una settimana di ferie a settembre.
lines.en: Yes. I would like to ask for a week of holiday in September.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quali giorni esattamente? A inizio mese siamo pochi.
lines.en: Which days exactly? At the start of the month we are short staffed.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pensavo dal cinque al dodici, ma posso spostare la partenza.
lines.en: I was thinking from the fifth to the twelfth, but I can move my departure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meglio la seconda metà del mese, se per lei va bene.
lines.en: The second half of the month is better, if that suits you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Va benissimo. Ho già un collega che mi sostituisce.
lines.en: That is fine. I already have a colleague who will cover for me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ottimo. Mi mandi la richiesta scritta e le do conferma.
lines.en: Excellent. Send me the written request and I will confirm it.

days.title: La scuola e la formazione in Italia
days.objective: Describe the stages of the Italian school system and talk about enrolling a child.
days.paretoFocus: Anyone with children, or anyone comparing their own qualifications, needs the names of the school stages, and they come up in every conversation about family.
drills: Name the school stages in order, with the ages.
Say which stage you or someone you know finished.
List what is usually needed to enrol a child.

items: What comes after la scuola primaria?
What is la maturità?
How do you say to enrol a child at school?

activities.title: Dalla primaria alla maturità
activities.intro: Children start with la scuola dell'infanzia, then la scuola primaria for five years, then la secondaria di primo grado for three and la secondaria di secondo grado for five. The final exam of the last stage is commonly called la maturità. Enrolment is called l'iscrizione and is done once a year.

items.hr: Mia figlia frequenta la scuola primaria da due anni.
items.en: My daughter has been going to primary school for two years.
items.note: frequentare = to attend

items.hr: Dopo la primaria si passa alla secondaria di primo grado.
items.en: After primary school you move on to lower secondary school.

items.hr: La secondaria di secondo grado dura cinque anni.
items.en: Upper secondary school lasts five years.

items.hr: Alla fine si fa l'esame di maturità.
items.en: At the end you sit the final school exam.
items.note: la maturità is the everyday name for the final exam

items.hr: Per l'iscrizione servono i documenti del bambino e la residenza.
items.en: For enrolment you need the child's documents and proof of residence.

activities.title: Il percorso scolastico
activities.intro: Practise the names of the school stages and the verbs used with them.

questions.prompt: The stage that comes straight after la scuola primaria is:
questions.explanation: The order is infanzia, primaria, secondaria di primo grado, secondaria di secondo grado.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct verb: mia figlia ___ la scuola primaria.
questions.explanation: Frequentare is the verb for attending a school or a course; assistere means to be present at an event.

questions.prompt: Complete with the everyday name of the final school exam: alla fine dei cinque anni si fa l'esame di ___ .
questions.explanation: La maturità closes the upper secondary stage and gives access to university.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for signing a child up at the start of the year: per l' ___ servono i documenti del bambino.
questions.explanation: L'iscrizione is the enrolment; the verb is iscrivere or iscriversi.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Upper secondary school lasts five years.'
questions.explanation: Durare is used with a length of time and takes no preposition before it.

activities.title: Iscrivere il bambino
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You ask a parent how the school stages work and what you need for enrolment.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quanti anni ha suo figlio? Deve iniziare quest'anno?
lines.en: How old is your son? Does he have to start this year?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ha sei anni. Comincia la primaria a settembre.
lines.en: He is six. He starts primary school in September.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bene. La primaria dura cinque anni, poi si passa alla secondaria.
lines.en: Good. Primary lasts five years, then you move on to secondary.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E per l'iscrizione che cosa devo portare?
lines.en: And for the enrolment what do I have to bring?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I documenti del bambino e un'autocertificazione della residenza.
lines.en: The child's documents and a self declaration of residence.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le iscrizioni si fanno in un periodo preciso dell'anno?
lines.en: Are enrolments done in a specific period of the year?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, di solito in inverno, per l'anno successivo.
lines.en: Yes, usually in winter, for the following year.

days.title: Trovare un corso di formazione
days.objective: Ask about a training or language course: level, timetable, cost and certificate.
days.paretoFocus: Evening courses are the cheapest way to add a qualification, and the same five questions get you the information for any of them.
drills: Ask five questions about a course you would like to take.
Describe your level and what you want to improve.
Ask whether the course ends with a certificate.

items: How do you ask when the course starts?
What is un attestato?
How do you say the course is free of charge?

activities.title: Informazioni sul corso
activities.intro: Ask about the level, the timetable and the cost. Un corso serale runs in the evening, and many training courses are free or heavily subsidised. At the end you may receive un attestato, a certificate of attendance, which is worth asking about before you enrol.

items.hr: Vorrei informazioni su un corso serale di informatica.
items.en: I would like information about an evening computer course.

items.hr: Il corso è gratuito oppure si paga una quota?
items.en: Is the course free or is there a fee to pay?
items.note: la quota = the fee

items.hr: Le lezioni sono due volte alla settimana, dalle diciotto alle venti.
items.en: The lessons are twice a week, from six to eight in the evening.

items.hr: Alla fine rilasciano un attestato di frequenza.
items.en: At the end they issue a certificate of attendance.

items.hr: Prima dell'iscrizione c'è un test per stabilire il livello.
items.en: Before enrolling there is a test to establish your level.

activities.title: Iscriversi a un corso
activities.intro: Practise asking about timetables, fees and certificates.

questions.prompt: Un corso serale is a course that takes place:
questions.explanation: Serale comes from sera, so the lessons are after working hours.

questions.prompt: Un attestato di frequenza certifies that:
questions.explanation: Frequenza means attendance, so the certificate records that you followed the course.

questions.prompt: Complete with the adjective meaning free of charge: il corso è ___ , non si paga niente.
questions.explanation: Gratuito is the adjective and gratis is the invariable adverb, both meaning free.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for the sum you pay to join: per partecipare si versa una ___ di cinquanta euro.
questions.explanation: La quota di iscrizione is the enrolment fee, and versare is the verb used for paying it.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The lessons are twice a week.'
questions.explanation: Frequency uses volte plus alla settimana, al mese or all'anno.

activities.title: Allo sportello del corso
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You ask for information about an evening course and decide to enrol.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buonasera. Cerca informazioni su qualche corso?
lines.en: Good evening. Are you looking for information about a course?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, vorrei sapere se c'è un corso serale di informatica.
lines.en: Yes, I would like to know if there is an evening computer course.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ne parte uno a ottobre, due sere alla settimana.
lines.en: One starts in October, two evenings a week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfetto per i miei orari. È gratuito o si paga una quota?
lines.en: Perfect for my hours. Is it free or is there a fee?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'è una quota bassa e alla fine rilasciamo un attestato.
lines.en: There is a small fee and at the end we issue a certificate.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Devo fare un test prima di iscrivermi?
lines.en: Do I have to take a test before enrolling?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Solo per i corsi di lingua. Per questo basta il modulo.
lines.en: Only for the language courses. For this one the form is enough.

days.title: Comprare i biglietti e cercare le coincidenze
days.objective: Buy a single or return ticket and ask about departure times, changes and connections.
days.paretoFocus: Ticket offices and machines run on a tiny fixed set of phrases, so learning them once covers every journey you will ever take.
drills: Say the same ticket request three ways out loud: one way, return, and two people travelling together.
Read the times 08:15, 14:35 and 19:50 aloud in Italian using the 24 hour convention.
Ask three questions about one imaginary journey: when it leaves, whether it is direct, and where you change.

items: How do you ask whether a train is direct or whether you must change?
What is the difference between di sola andata and di andata e ritorno?
Which verb do Italians use for missing a connection, and what is its participle?

activities.title: Alla biglietteria
activities.intro: Buying a ticket in Italian is built on vorrei plus the ticket type, then a question about the time or the change. The word coincidenza means the connecting service, not a coincidence. Keep the article with each noun: il biglietto, la coincidenza, il binario.

items.hr: Vorrei un biglietto di sola andata per Bologna, per favore.
items.en: I would like a one way ticket to Bologna, please.
items.note: di sola andata = one way; per + city = to that city

items.hr: Il biglietto di andata e ritorno costa un po' meno.
items.en: The return ticket costs a little less.
items.note: andata e ritorno = return, literally going and coming back

items.hr: A che ora parte il primo treno del mattino?
items.en: What time does the first morning train leave?
items.note: a che ora + verb is the standard time question

items.hr: Il treno è diretto o devo cambiare?
items.en: Is the train direct or do I have to change?
items.note: cambiare on its own already means to change trains

items.hr: Ho perso la coincidenza delle 14:35.
items.en: I missed the 14:35 connection.
items.note: perdere -> perso; times take delle plus the 24 hour figure

activities.title: Un biglietto, per favore
activities.intro: Practise asking for tickets and checking connections.

questions.prompt: Which sentence asks whether you have to change trains?
questions.explanation: Cambiare treno is the fixed expression for changing services. Comprare means to buy and perdere means to miss.

questions.prompt: You want a return ticket. Which one do you ask for?
options: un biglietto di andata e ritorno
questions.answer: un biglietto di andata e ritorno
questions.explanation: Andata e ritorno covers both legs of the journey. Di sola andata is one way only.

questions.prompt: Complete the request for a ticket with no return leg: 'Vorrei un biglietto di sola ______ per Firenze.'
questions.explanation: The set phrase is di sola andata, where andata is the outward leg.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past participle: 'Ho ______ la coincidenza per Napoli.' (the verb is perdere)
questions.explanation: Perdere has the irregular participle perso, though perduto also exists and is more formal.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The next train leaves at half past two in the afternoon.'
questions.explanation: Prossimo goes before the noun here, and clock times take alle plus the figure.

activities.title: Due biglietti per domani
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are buying two tickets for tomorrow and you need to know whether there is a change.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, mi dica.
lines.en: Good morning, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno, vorrei due biglietti di andata e ritorno per Verona, per domani.
lines.en: Good morning, I would like two return tickets to Verona, for tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'è un treno alle 9:10 e uno alle 11:40. Quale preferisce?
lines.en: There is a train at 9:10 and one at 11:40. Which do you prefer?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quello delle 9:10. È diretto o devo cambiare?
lines.en: The 9:10 one. Is it direct or do I have to change?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Deve cambiare a Padova, ma la coincidenza parte dopo dieci minuti.
lines.en: You have to change at Padua, but the connection leaves ten minutes later.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Va bene. E da quale binario parte?
lines.en: All right. And which platform does it leave from?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dal binario 7. Sono trentotto euro in tutto.
lines.en: From platform 7. That is thirty-eight euros in total.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ecco. Grazie mille e buona giornata.
lines.en: Here you are. Thank you very much and have a good day.

days.title: In stazione, ritardi e coincidenze
days.objective: Understand and react to delays, cancellations and platform changes announced in a station.
days.paretoFocus: Announcements reuse the same handful of nouns and passive forms, so decoding them turns a stressful moment into a routine one.
drills: Say aloud three delays of different lengths using in ritardo di plus the number of minutes.
Turn one cancelled departure into a plan B sentence beginning with allora prendo.
Listen to any Italian audio and write down every number you hear as a figure.

items: How do you say a train is twenty minutes late?
Which preposition goes with binario when a train departs from it?
What does the announcement mean when it says il treno è stato cancellato?

activities.title: Annunci e ritardi
activities.intro: Station language is short and repetitive. Delays use essere in ritardo di plus a time, platforms use dal binario, and cancellations use the passive è stato cancellato. Notice that the participle agrees with the subject in the passive.

items.hr: Il treno per Milano è in ritardo di venti minuti.
items.en: The train to Milan is twenty minutes late.
items.note: in ritardo di + time; the opposite is in anticipo

items.hr: Il regionale delle 9:40 è stato cancellato.
items.en: The 9:40 regional train has been cancelled.
items.note: essere stato + participle = passive; cancellato agrees with il regionale

items.hr: Il treno parte dal binario 7, non dal binario 3.
items.en: The train leaves from platform 7, not from platform 3.
items.note: da + il = dal

items.hr: Se perdo la coincidenza, prendo il treno successivo.
items.en: If I miss the connection, I will take the next train.
items.note: se + present, present: the normal everyday conditional

items.hr: La sala d'attesa è vicino all'uscita principale.
items.en: The waiting room is near the main exit.
items.note: vicino a + il = vicino al, + la = vicino alla, + l' = vicino all'

activities.title: Che cosa dice l'annuncio
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of delays, platforms and cancellations.

questions.prompt: Which sentence tells you the train will not run at all?
questions.explanation: Cancellato means cancelled. In ritardo only means late, and in arrivo means it is arriving.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: '___ binario 12 parte il treno per Roma.'
questions.explanation: Departure takes da, and da plus il contracts to dal.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Il treno è in ______ di trenta minuti.' (it is not on time, it is late)
questions.explanation: Essere in ritardo is the fixed expression for being late; the amount follows with di.

questions.prompt: Make the participle agree: 'Le due corse serali sono state ______.' (from cancellare)
questions.explanation: In the passive with essere the participle agrees with the subject, and le corse is feminine plural, so cancellate.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'If I miss the connection, I will take the next train.'
questions.explanation: Everyday Italian uses the present in both halves of a real condition.

activities.title: Il treno è in ritardo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your train is late and you are worried about the connection you booked.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Attenzione: il treno delle 15:20 viaggia con venti minuti di ritardo.
lines.en: Attention: the 15:20 train is running twenty minutes late.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Scusi, con questo ritardo perdo la coincidenza per Bari?
lines.en: Excuse me, with this delay will I miss the connection to Bari?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Probabilmente sì, ma c'è un altro treno un'ora dopo.
lines.en: Probably yes, but there is another train an hour later.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Devo comprare un altro biglietto o vale questo?
lines.en: Do I have to buy another ticket or is this one valid?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vale questo, il ritardo non è colpa sua.
lines.en: This one is valid, the delay is not your fault.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Meno male. E da quale binario parte il treno delle 16:30?
lines.en: Thank goodness. And which platform does the 16:30 train leave from?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dal binario 4, ma controlli il tabellone prima di salire.
lines.en: From platform 4, but check the board before getting on.

days.title: In aeroporto
days.objective: Check in, deal with baggage and understand boarding information at an airport.
days.paretoFocus: Airport words are near universal but the Italian ones are false friends often enough that knowing them prevents the one mistake that costs a flight.
drills: Name out loud five things you carry in your hand luggage, each with its article.
Describe your suitcase in one sentence: colour, size and any label on it.
Say what time boarding starts and what time the flight leaves, using the 24 hour clock.

items: What is the Italian for hand luggage, and which article does it take?
Which two verbs describe getting on a plane and getting off it?
How do you say your flight is delayed because of the weather?

activities.title: Il check-in e l'imbarco
activities.intro: Airport Italian centres on il volo, il bagaglio and l'imbarco. Note that il bagaglio is singular for the whole of your luggage, while una valigia is one case. The gate is l'uscita or il gate, and both are heard.

items.hr: Devo fare il check-in e imbarcare la valigia.
items.en: I have to check in and put my suitcase in the hold.
items.note: fare il check-in is the fixed phrase; imbarcare here means to send to the hold

items.hr: Il bagaglio a mano deve stare sotto il sedile.
items.en: Hand luggage has to go under the seat.
items.note: il bagaglio a mano, invariable phrase

items.hr: L'imbarco comincia alle 16:20 all'uscita 12.
items.en: Boarding starts at 16:20 at gate 12.
items.note: l'imbarco takes l' because imbarco begins with a vowel

items.hr: Ho dimenticato la carta d'imbarco sul telefono.
items.en: I left the boarding pass on my phone.
items.note: la carta d'imbarco = boarding pass

items.hr: Il volo è in ritardo per il maltempo.
items.en: The flight is delayed because of the bad weather.
items.note: per + noun gives the cause, like because of

activities.title: Prima di partire
activities.intro: Practise airport nouns and the verbs that go with them.

questions.prompt: Which phrase means hand luggage?
questions.explanation: A mano marks the bag you keep with you; il deposito bagagli is a left luggage office.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct article: '___ imbarco comincia fra dieci minuti.'
questions.explanation: Imbarco is masculine but starts with a vowel, so il becomes l'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the document you show at the gate: 'Mi dia la sua carta d'______, per favore.'
questions.explanation: La carta d'imbarco is the boarding pass; the apostrophe replaces the i of di.

questions.prompt: Complete with the cause: 'Il volo è stato cancellato ______ la nebbia.' (one word meaning because of)
questions.explanation: Per plus a noun expresses cause in Italian, where English uses because of.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I have to check in and put my suitcase in the hold.'
questions.explanation: Dovere is followed by the bare infinitive, and both infinitives hang off the same devo.

activities.title: Al banco del check-in
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are checking in one case and asking about the gate.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buonasera, documento e prenotazione, per favore.
lines.en: Good evening, document and booking, please.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Eccoli. Vorrei imbarcare questa valigia.
lines.en: Here they are. I would like to check this suitcase in.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene, pesa ventidue chili. E il bagaglio a mano?
lines.en: That is fine, it weighs twenty-two kilos. And your hand luggage?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ho solo questo zaino, lo tengo con me.
lines.en: I only have this backpack, I am keeping it with me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfetto. L'imbarco è alle 19:45 all'uscita 22.
lines.en: Perfect. Boarding is at 19:45 at gate 22.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il volo parte in orario o è in ritardo?
lines.en: Is the flight leaving on time or is it delayed?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Per ora è in orario. Buon viaggio!
lines.en: For now it is on time. Have a good trip!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grazie, buona serata.
lines.en: Thank you, have a good evening.

days.title: L'albergo, prenotare e fare il check-in
days.objective: Book a room by phone or in person and ask what is included in the price.
days.paretoFocus: The booking exchange is short and always the same, so a dozen memorised lines let you handle any stay from start to finish.
drills: Book three different rooms out loud: a single for one night, a double for three, a triple for a week.
List five things a room might have, each with its article, and say which ones matter to you.
Ask two questions about the price: what is included and whether breakfast costs extra.

items: How do you say a double room and a single room?
Which question asks whether breakfast comes with the room?
How do you say the booking is in someone's name?

activities.title: Prenotare una camera
activities.intro: A room is la camera, and its type follows as an adjective: singola, doppia, matrimoniale. Prices use a notte for per night, and compreso means included, agreeing with what is included.

items.hr: Vorrei prenotare una camera doppia per tre notti.
items.en: I would like to book a double room for three nights.
items.note: per + number + notti gives the length of stay

items.hr: Quanto costa a notte, colazione compresa?
items.en: How much is it per night, breakfast included?
items.note: compresa is feminine because la colazione is feminine

items.hr: Ho prenotato a nome di Marco Bianchi.
items.en: I booked under the name Marco Bianchi.
items.note: a nome di + name, fixed phrase at reception

items.hr: A che ora dobbiamo lasciare la camera?
items.en: What time do we have to leave the room?
items.note: lasciare la camera is what Italians say for checking out

items.hr: La camera è al secondo piano, l'ascensore è a destra.
items.en: The room is on the second floor, the lift is on the right.
items.note: al + ordinal + piano for floors

activities.title: Alla reception
activities.intro: Practise booking a room and asking what the price covers.

questions.prompt: You are travelling alone. Which room do you ask for?
questions.explanation: Singola is for one person. Matrimoniale has one large bed for two, doppia has two beds.

questions.prompt: Which question asks how much one night costs?
questions.explanation: Quanto asks how much, and a notte is the fixed phrase for per night.

questions.prompt: Make the adjective agree: 'Il prezzo è di ottanta euro, colazione ______.' (from compreso)
questions.explanation: The adjective agrees with la colazione, which is feminine singular, so compresa.

questions.prompt: Complete with the plural of la notte: 'Vorrei una camera singola per quattro ______.'
questions.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in e form the plural in i, so la notte becomes le notti.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I would like to book a double room for three nights.'
questions.explanation: The adjective doppia follows the noun, and per introduces the length of stay.

activities.title: Abbiamo una prenotazione
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You arrive at reception with a booking and check the practical details.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buonasera, ha una prenotazione?
lines.en: Good evening, do you have a booking?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, ho prenotato a nome di Conti, una camera doppia per due notti.
lines.en: Yes, I booked under the name Conti, a double room for two nights.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfetto. Camera 24, al secondo piano.
lines.en: Perfect. Room 24, on the second floor.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La colazione è compresa nel prezzo?
lines.en: Is breakfast included in the price?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, dalle 7:00 alle 10:00 nella sala al piano terra.
lines.en: Yes, from 7:00 to 10:00 in the room on the ground floor.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E a che ora dobbiamo lasciare la camera l'ultimo giorno?
lines.en: And what time do we have to leave the room on the last day?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Entro le 11:00. Ecco le chiavi, buon soggiorno.
lines.en: By 11:00. Here are the keys, enjoy your stay.

days.title: Lamentarsi quando qualcosa non va
days.objective: Complain politely and clearly when something is broken, wrong or late.
days.paretoFocus: Complaining is the moment learners fall back on their own language, and a few polite formulas get the problem solved without sounding rude.
drills: Think of three things in your home that do not work and say each one with non funziona.
Turn one blunt complaint into a polite one by opening with mi scusi and using the conditional.
Say how long you waited for something recently using ho aspettato plus a time.

items: Which verb do you use for something that is broken and will not work?
How do you politely ask to speak to the person in charge?
How do you say there is a mistake in the bill?

activities.title: Scusi, c'è un problema
activities.intro: Italian complaints stay polite by opening with mi scusi and by using the conditional vorrei instead of voglio. Non funziona covers anything broken, and c'è un errore covers anything wrong on paper. Say the problem, then say what you want done.

items.hr: Mi scusi, il riscaldamento non funziona.
items.en: Excuse me, the heating does not work.
items.note: non funziona for any device, appliance or system

items.hr: Ho aspettato più di mezz'ora.
items.en: I waited more than half an hour.
items.note: più takes an accent; mezz'ora with the apostrophe

items.hr: Guardi, c'è un errore nel conto.
items.en: Look, there is a mistake in the bill.
items.note: guardi is the polite Lei imperative and softens the news

items.hr: Vorrei parlare con il responsabile, per favore.
items.en: I would like to speak to the person in charge, please.
items.note: vorrei softens the request; voglio sounds aggressive

items.hr: Non è quello che ho ordinato.
items.en: This is not what I ordered.
items.note: quello che = what, the thing that

activities.title: Un reclamo educato
activities.intro: Practise reporting a problem and asking for it to be put right.

questions.prompt: The air conditioning is broken. Which sentence do you say?
questions.explanation: Funzionare is the verb for machines working. Servire means to be useful, not to operate.

questions.prompt: Which opening sounds politest to a member of staff you do not know?
questions.explanation: Mi scusi is the Lei form and the conditional avrei softens the complaint. Senti and tu are informal.

questions.prompt: Complete with the polite conditional of volere: '______ parlare con il responsabile.' (I would like)
questions.explanation: Vorrei is the first person conditional of volere and is the standard polite request form.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ho aspettato ______ di mezz'ora.' (the word meaning more, with its accent)
questions.explanation: Più always carries the grave accent on the u, and più di goes before a quantity.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Excuse me, this is not what I ordered.'
questions.explanation: Quello che introduces the thing that, and the passato prossimo follows it normally.

activities.title: In camera non funziona niente
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Two things in your room are wrong and you report them politely.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Reception, buonasera. Mi dica.
lines.en: Reception, good evening. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buonasera, chiamo dalla 24. Il riscaldamento non funziona.
lines.en: Good evening, I am calling from room 24. The heating does not work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi dispiace molto. Mando subito qualcuno a controllare.
lines.en: I am very sorry. I will send someone up to check right away.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grazie. C'è anche un altro problema: manca l'acqua calda.
lines.en: Thank you. There is another problem too: there is no hot water.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Anche quello? Le chiedo scusa, vediamo cosa possiamo fare.
lines.en: That as well? I apologise, we will see what we can do.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se non si risolve, vorrei cambiare camera.
lines.en: If it is not sorted out, I would like to change rooms.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo, abbiamo una camera libera al terzo piano.
lines.en: Of course, we have a free room on the third floor.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfetto, la prendo. Grazie della disponibilità.
lines.en: Perfect, I will take it. Thank you for your help.

days.title: Cambiare o restituire un acquisto
days.objective: Exchange or return something you bought and explain what is wrong with it.
days.paretoFocus: Returning goods needs precise words for size, fault and refund, and getting them right decides whether you get your money back.
drills: Describe three items of clothing you own with size and colour, each with its article.
Say the same complaint two ways: too small, and one size bigger needed.
Practise the sentence you would say handing over a receipt and asking for a refund.

items: What is the Italian for a receipt you get in a shop?
How do you ask for the same item in a bigger size?
Which noun means a refund, and which verb goes with it?

activities.title: Vorrei cambiare questo
activities.intro: Cambiare means to exchange, restituire means to give back, and il rimborso is the money returned. Clothes sizes use la taglia for clothing and il numero for shoes. Notice that adjectives of size agree with the garment.

items.hr: Vorrei cambiare questa maglietta, è troppo stretta.
items.en: I would like to exchange this t-shirt, it is too tight.
items.note: stretta agrees with la maglietta; the opposite is larga

items.hr: Posso restituirla e avere il rimborso?
items.en: Can I return it and get a refund?
items.note: restituirla = restituire + la, the pronoun attaches to the infinitive

items.hr: Ecco lo scontrino, l'ho comprata sabato.
items.en: Here is the receipt, I bought it on Saturday.
items.note: lo scontrino takes lo because of s + consonant

items.hr: Questo maglione ha un difetto, la manica è scucita.
items.en: This jumper has a fault, the sleeve is coming apart.
items.note: il difetto = fault or flaw

items.hr: Avete la stessa camicia in una taglia più grande?
items.en: Do you have the same shirt in a bigger size?
items.note: la taglia for clothes, il numero for shoes

activities.title: Al banco dei resi
activities.intro: Practise the language of exchanges, faults and refunds.

questions.prompt: You want your money back, not another item. What do you ask for?
questions.explanation: Il rimborso is a refund. Il resto is the change you get back after paying, and lo sconto is a discount.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct article: '___ scontrino è nella borsa.'
questions.explanation: Masculine nouns starting with s plus a consonant take lo, so lo scontrino.

questions.prompt: Make the adjective agree: 'Questa gonna è troppo ______.' (from stretto, meaning tight)
questions.explanation: La gonna is feminine singular, so the adjective ends in a.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for clothing size: 'Avete questa camicia in una ______ più grande?'
questions.explanation: La taglia is used for clothes; shoes use il numero instead.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Can I return it and get a refund?'
questions.explanation: After a modal verb the object pronoun attaches to the end of the infinitive, and restituire loses its final e.

activities.title: Questa maglietta non va bene
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are exchanging a t-shirt that is the wrong size.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, desidera?
lines.en: Good morning, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno, vorrei cambiare questa maglietta, è troppo stretta.
lines.en: Good morning, I would like to exchange this t-shirt, it is too tight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha lo scontrino?
lines.en: Do you have the receipt?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, eccolo. L'ho comprata martedì.
lines.en: Yes, here it is. I bought it on Tuesday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nessun problema. La vuole in una taglia più grande?
lines.en: No problem. Would you like it one size bigger?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, e se possibile in blu invece che in nero.
lines.en: Yes, and if possible in blue instead of black.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Controllo subito in magazzino, un attimo.
lines.en: I will check in the stockroom right away, one moment.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grazie. Se non c'è, preferisco il rimborso.
lines.en: Thank you. If there is none, I would prefer a refund.

days.title: Raccontare un viaggio
days.objective: Tell the story of a past trip, mixing completed events with background description.
days.paretoFocus: Telling a trip story is the most common long turn people take in conversation, and it forces the passato prossimo and imperfetto to work together.
drills: Tell a real trip in five sentences: where, when, who with, one event, one opinion.
Take each sentence and add one background detail in the imperfetto about the weather or the place.
Retell the same trip starting from the last day and working backwards.

items: Which past tense do you use for a single completed event, and which for background?
Which auxiliary does andare take, and what happens to the participle?
How do you say last summer and two years ago?

activities.title: Il viaggio dell'anno scorso
activities.intro: A trip story alternates two tenses. The passato prossimo carries the events that moved the story forward, and the imperfetto paints the scenery, the weather and how things felt. Verbs of movement take essere, so the participle agrees with the subject.

items.hr: L'estate scorsa siamo andati in Sicilia in treno.
items.en: Last summer we went to Sicily by train.
items.note: andare takes essere, so andati agrees with the plural subject

items.hr: Faceva molto caldo e il mare era caldissimo.
items.en: It was very hot and the sea was really warm.
items.note: imperfetto for weather and lasting states

items.hr: Abbiamo visitato tre città in una settimana.
items.en: We visited three cities in one week.
items.note: città is invariable and keeps its accent in the plural

items.hr: Mentre aspettavamo il traghetto, abbiamo mangiato qualcosa.
items.en: While we were waiting for the ferry, we ate something.
items.note: mentre always takes the imperfetto

items.hr: È stato un viaggio faticoso ma bellissimo.
items.en: It was a tiring but wonderful trip.
items.note: è stato for a closed judgement on the whole trip

activities.title: Com'è andato il viaggio
activities.intro: Practise choosing between the two past tenses inside one story.

questions.prompt: Choose the right tense: 'Mentre ___ in spiaggia, ha cominciato a piovere.'
questions.explanation: Mentre describes an ongoing background situation, which always takes the imperfetto.

questions.prompt: Which sentence describes the weather during the whole holiday?
options: Ha fatto brutto tempo un giorno.
Faceva bel tempo tutti i giorni.
questions.answer: Faceva bel tempo tutti i giorni.
questions.explanation: A continuing weather condition across the trip is background, so it takes the imperfetto faceva.

questions.prompt: Make the participle agree: 'Mia sorella e io siamo ______ in Puglia in agosto.' (from andare)
questions.explanation: Andare takes essere, and with two feminine subjects the participle is feminine plural, andate.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfetto of essere: 'Il mare ______ caldissimo quel giorno.'
questions.explanation: Era is the third person singular imperfetto of essere, used here for a lasting state.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'It was a tiring but wonderful trip.'
questions.explanation: Essere in the passato prossimo is è stato, and both adjectives follow the noun.

activities.title: Raccontami le vacanze
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about your holiday and you narrate it with both past tenses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora, com'è andata la vacanza?
lines.en: So, how was the holiday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Benissimo. Siamo andati in Sicilia in treno, un viaggio lunghissimo.
lines.en: Really well. We went to Sicily by train, a very long journey.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E che tempo faceva?
lines.en: And what was the weather like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Faceva un caldo terribile, ma il mare era perfetto.
lines.en: It was terribly hot, but the sea was perfect.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Avete visitato molti posti?
lines.en: Did you visit many places?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tre città in una settimana. Mentre aspettavamo il traghetto, abbiamo conosciuto una coppia simpaticissima.
lines.en: Three cities in one week. While we were waiting for the ferry, we met a really nice couple.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Che bello. Ci tornereste?
lines.en: How lovely. Would you go back?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Subito, ma la prossima volta non in agosto.
lines.en: Straight away, but next time not in August.

days.title: Le email formali e informali
days.objective: Write the same short message twice, once formally with Lei and once informally with tu.
days.paretoFocus: Register is exactly what a written A2 task is marked on, and the openings and closings alone signal formality before a single sentence of content.
drills: Write one three line message asking to move an appointment, first formally, then informally.
List the four formal openings and closings and the four informal ones, in pairs.
Take any informal message you have written and rewrite every tu form as Lei.

items: Which opening and closing pair goes with a person you address as Lei?
What changes in the verb forms when you switch from tu to Lei?
Which closing would be wrong in a message to an office, and why?

activities.title: Gentile Dottoressa, Ciao Anna
activities.intro: An Italian message is formal or informal from the first word to the last. Formally you open with Gentile plus a title, use Lei all the way through, and close with Cordiali saluti. Informally you open with Ciao, use tu, and close with something warm like Un abbraccio. The five items below are the same message written both ways.

items.hr: Gentile Dottoressa Rossi, Le scrivo per chiedere un appuntamento.
items.en: Dear Dr Rossi, I am writing to you to ask for an appointment.
items.note: formal opening; Le is the Lei indirect pronoun and is often capitalised

items.hr: Ciao Anna, ti scrivo per chiederti se hai tempo giovedì.
items.en: Hi Anna, I am writing to ask you whether you have time on Thursday.
items.note: same message, informal: ti and hai instead of Le and ha

items.hr: La ringrazio in anticipo e resto in attesa di una Sua risposta.
items.en: I thank you in advance and I look forward to your reply.
items.note: formal body stays in Lei; La and Sua are capitalised out of courtesy

items.hr: Fammi sapere se ti va, così ci organizziamo.
items.en: Let me know if you fancy it, so we can sort things out.
items.note: informal body: fammi is the tu imperative of fare plus mi

items.hr: Cordiali saluti, Marco Bianchi. / Un abbraccio, Marco.
items.en: Kind regards, Marco Bianchi. / A hug, Marco.
items.note: formal closings sign with the full name, informal ones with the first name only

activities.title: Formale o informale
activities.intro: Practise matching openings, pronouns and closings to the right register.

questions.prompt: Which closing belongs at the end of a message to an office you have never visited?
questions.explanation: Cordiali saluti is the neutral formal closing. The other three are for friends and family.

questions.prompt: Which sentence keeps the formal register all the way through?
options: Gentile Dottore, ti scrivo per un appuntamento.
Gentile Dottore, Le scrivo per un appuntamento.
Ciao Dottore, Le scrivo per un appuntamento.
questions.answer: Gentile Dottore, Le scrivo per un appuntamento.
questions.explanation: A formal opening must be followed by Lei forms. Mixing Gentile with ti or fammi breaks the register.

questions.prompt: Complete the formal opening word before a title: '______ Dottoressa Rossi, La ringrazio della risposta.'
questions.explanation: Gentile plus a title is the standard formal opening, and it does not change for masculine or feminine.

questions.prompt: Rewrite in the formal register: 'Ti scrivo per un favore.' becomes '______ scrivo per un favore.'
questions.explanation: The indirect object pronoun for Lei is Le, capitalised in careful writing, and it replaces the informal ti.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Dear Doctor, I am writing to you to ask for an appointment.'
questions.explanation: The pronoun Le comes before the conjugated verb, and per plus the infinitive gives the purpose.

activities.title: Come lo scrivo?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend checks how you should word a message to an office.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Devi scrivere alla segreteria? Come cominci?
lines.en: Do you have to write to the office? How are you starting?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Con Gentile Dottoressa Rossi, perché non la conosco.
lines.en: With Dear Dr Rossi, because I do not know her.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Giusto. E dentro usi il tu o il Lei?
lines.en: Right. And inside do you use tu or Lei?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il Lei dall'inizio alla fine: Le scrivo per chiedere un appuntamento.
lines.en: Lei from beginning to end: I am writing to you to ask for an appointment.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E come chiudi?
lines.en: And how do you close?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cordiali saluti e il nome e cognome. Un abbraccio lo scrivo solo a te.
lines.en: Kind regards and my first and last name. A hug I only write to you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfetto, hai capito tutto.
lines.en: Perfect, you have understood everything.

days.title: Telefonare, presentarsi e passare la chiamata
days.objective: Make and answer a phone call, say who you are and ask to be put through.
days.paretoFocus: On the phone you lose faces and gestures, so the fixed opening lines are what keep the conversation from collapsing in the first ten seconds.
drills: Answer an imaginary call three times: at home, at work, and to a wrong number.
Give your name and the person you need, in one breath, five times.
Leave a twenty second message giving your name, your reason and your number.

items: Which single word do Italians say when they pick up the phone?
How do you say who is calling, and how do you ask who is calling?
How do you offer to put someone through to a colleague?

activities.title: Pronto, chi parla?
activities.intro: Pronto is only used on the phone, by the person answering. To identify yourself you say sono plus your name, with no article. Passare means to put a call through, and the combined pronoun glielo passo means I am putting him on to her or him.

items.hr: Pronto, sono Giulia. Posso parlare con il signor Neri?
items.en: Hello, this is Giulia. Can I speak to Mr Neri?
items.note: sono + name, never io sono chiamato

items.hr: Chi parla, scusi?
items.en: Who is calling, sorry?
items.note: literally who speaks, the standard way to ask

items.hr: Attenda in linea, glielo passo subito.
items.en: Hold the line, I am putting him through to you right away.
items.note: glielo = gli/le + lo, the combined pronoun

items.hr: Mi dispiace, in questo momento non c'è. Vuole lasciare un messaggio?
items.en: I am sorry, he is not here at the moment. Would you like to leave a message?
items.note: non c'è for someone not being in

items.hr: Mi scusi, ha sbagliato numero.
items.en: Sorry, you have got the wrong number.
items.note: sbagliare numero, no preposition

activities.title: Al telefono
activities.intro: Practise the fixed phrases of an Italian phone call.

questions.prompt: Which word does the person answering the phone say first?
questions.explanation: Pronto is used only when picking up the phone. The others are ordinary greetings used face to face.

questions.prompt: How do you say who you are on the phone?
questions.explanation: Essere plus the bare name is the phone formula; mi chiamo Giulia is also correct but never takes di.

questions.prompt: Complete the question asking for the caller's identity: 'Chi ______, scusi?'
questions.explanation: Italians ask who is speaking, using the third person singular of parlare.

questions.prompt: Complete with the combined pronoun meaning it to him: 'Attenda un attimo, ______ passo.'
questions.explanation: Gli plus lo combine into the single form glielo, written as one word.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Sorry, you have got the wrong number.'
questions.explanation: Sbagliare numero takes no article and no preposition in this fixed expression.

activities.title: Posso parlare con il dottore?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You call an office, give your name and request the right department.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pronto, buongiorno.
lines.en: Hello, good morning.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno, sono Marco Conti. Posso parlare con la dottoressa Rossi?
lines.en: Good morning, this is Marco Conti. Can I speak to Dr Rossi?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Un attimo, vedo se è in ufficio. Attenda in linea.
lines.en: One moment, I will see if she is in the office. Hold the line.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certo, grazie.
lines.en: Of course, thank you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi dispiace, adesso non c'è. Vuole lasciare un messaggio?
lines.en: I am sorry, she is not in right now. Would you like to leave a message?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, le dica che ho chiamato e che richiamo domani mattina.
lines.en: Yes, tell her that I called and that I will call back tomorrow morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene. Mi lascia il suo numero?
lines.en: All right. Will you leave me your number?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certo, glielo do subito: 340 5567788.
lines.en: Of course, I will give it to you right away: 340 5567788.

days.title: Accettare o rifiutare un invito
days.objective: Invite someone out, accept warmly, or refuse without giving offence.
days.paretoFocus: Turning something down politely is harder than saying yes, and Italian has set softeners that make a refusal sound friendly rather than cold.
drills: Invite a friend to three different things using ti va di plus an infinitive.
Refuse each invitation with a reason and an offer of another time.
Accept one invitation and immediately ask two practical questions about it.

items: Which structure invites someone using an infinitive after it?
What is the one word enthusiastic yes to an invitation?
Which phrase softens a refusal by suggesting another occasion?

activities.title: Ti va di uscire?
activities.intro: Invitations use ti va di plus an infinitive, or the simpler vieni a. A refusal needs three parts: the apology, the reason, and the door left open. Leaving out the reason sounds rude in Italian, even between friends.

items.hr: Ti va di venire a cena da noi sabato?
items.en: Do you fancy coming to dinner at our place on Saturday?
items.note: ti va di + infinitive; da noi = at our place

items.hr: Volentieri! A che ora vi trovate?
items.en: Gladly! What time are you meeting?
items.note: volentieri is the standard enthusiastic yes

items.hr: Mi dispiace, sabato non posso, ho già un impegno.
items.en: I am sorry, I cannot on Saturday, I already have something on.
items.note: un impegno = a commitment, the polite catch-all reason

items.hr: Magari un'altra volta, mi farebbe piacere.
items.en: Maybe another time, I would like that.
items.note: un'altra with the apostrophe, because altra is feminine

items.hr: Grazie dell'invito, ci sarò sicuramente.
items.en: Thanks for the invitation, I will definitely be there.
items.note: grazie di + article; sarò is the future of essere

activities.title: Sì, grazie, o no, grazie
activities.intro: Practise inviting, accepting and turning down invitations.

questions.prompt: Which reply accepts an invitation enthusiastically in one word?
questions.explanation: Volentieri means gladly. Purtroppo introduces bad news and dipende means it depends.

questions.prompt: Which refusal leaves the door open for another occasion?
questions.explanation: Magari un'altra volta softens the no by proposing a future occasion. The others close the conversation.

questions.prompt: Complete the invitation structure: 'Ti va ______ andare al cinema stasera?'
questions.explanation: The fixed pattern is ti va di plus the infinitive, never ti va a.

questions.prompt: Complete with the apostrophe form: 'Magari un'______ volta, questa settimana sono pieno.'
questions.explanation: Una becomes un' before a feminine word beginning with a vowel, giving un'altra volta.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am sorry, I cannot on Saturday, I already have something on.'
questions.explanation: Già sits between the auxiliary or verb and its object, and non posso can stand alone without an infinitive.

activities.title: Una cena sabato sera
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are invited to dinner, you cannot go, and you suggest an alternative.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Senti, ti va di venire a cena da noi sabato?
lines.en: Listen, do you fancy coming to dinner at ours on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Che bello, grazie dell'invito! Ma sabato purtroppo non posso.
lines.en: How lovely, thanks for the invitation! But unfortunately I cannot on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Peccato. Hai già un impegno?
lines.en: What a shame. Do you already have something on?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, è il compleanno di mia madre e siamo tutti da lei.
lines.en: Yes, it is my mother's birthday and we are all at her place.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Capisco, allora facciamo un'altra volta.
lines.en: I understand, then we will do it another time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Volentieri. Domenica prossima io sono libero, se vi va.
lines.en: Gladly. Next Sunday I am free, if you fancy it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfetto, domenica allora. Ti scrivo l'ora.
lines.en: Perfect, Sunday then. I will message you the time.

days.title: Auguri e condoglianze
days.objective: Say the right set phrase for birthdays, exams, celebrations and bereavements.
days.paretoFocus: These are frozen expressions that cannot be translated word for word, and using the wrong one is noticed immediately.
drills: Match a greeting to five occasions: a birthday, an exam, a wedding, a new year, a funeral.
Say the good luck phrase and its obligatory reply out loud until both are automatic.
Write one two line message of congratulations and one of condolence.

items: What do Italians say instead of good luck, and what is the fixed reply?
Which single word covers best wishes on almost any happy occasion?
Which phrase do you use to express sympathy after a death?

activities.title: Auguri e in bocca al lupo
activities.intro: Auguri is the all purpose good wish, used for birthdays, name days and holidays. For exams and challenges Italians say in bocca al lupo, and the reply is always crepi. Condoglianze is formal and is never shortened.

items.hr: Buon compleanno! Cento di questi giorni!
items.en: Happy birthday! Many happy returns!
items.note: cento di questi giorni is the traditional birthday wish

items.hr: Auguri di buon Natale e felice anno nuovo.
items.en: Best wishes for a merry Christmas and a happy new year.
items.note: auguri di + occasion in written greetings

items.hr: Congratulazioni per la laurea, te lo meriti!
items.en: Congratulations on your degree, you deserve it!
items.note: congratulazioni for an achievement, auguri for an occasion

items.hr: In bocca al lupo per l'esame di domani. Crepi!
items.en: Good luck for tomorrow's exam. Thanks!
items.note: the reply crepi is obligatory; buona fortuna sounds odd here

items.hr: Le porgo le mie più sentite condoglianze.
items.en: I offer you my deepest condolences.
items.note: formal Lei; più keeps its accent

activities.title: La frase giusta
